name: GEM Global Active Faults Database (GAF-DB)
provider: Global Earthquake Model Foundation
theme_raw: active fault traces; seismic hazard source faults
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
formats: GeoJSON, GeoPackage, KML, ESRI Shapefile, GMT
access: GitHub repository; geoviewer; direct file download
doi_or_citation: Styron, R. and Pagani, M. (2020), The GEM Global Active Faults Database, Earthquake Spectra, doi:10.1177/8755293020944182
time_span: compiled active/neotectonic faults; repository latest public release 2019 plus maintained source tree
notes: Backbone global active-fault compilation; README lists constituent regional datasets and states coverage exceptions including Malay Archipelago, Madagascar, Canada, and a few other regions.
desc_zh: GEM GAF-DB 是全球活动断层骨架数据集，面向地震危险性建模整理活动断层迹线、几何、运动学、滑动速率和参考文献等属性。数据在 GitHub 开放，提供 GeoJSON、GeoPackage、KML、Shapefile 和 GMT 等格式，适合作为全球尺度断层图谱的一级索引。需要注意其区域覆盖仍有缺口，且许多区域来自二级汇编。
desc_en: The GEM Global Active Faults Database is a global backbone compilation of active fault traces for seismic hazard modeling and tectonic applications. It exposes geometry, kinematics, slip-rate fields, references, and quality attributes through a public GitHub repository in GeoJSON, GeoPackage, KML, shapefile, and GMT formats. It is the best first-pass global index, but regional completeness varies and several areas are still inherited from heterogeneous source compilations.
feature_count: about 13,500 faults reported in publication abstract; repository evolves

name: Quaternary Fault and Fold Database of the United States
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Earthquake Hazards Program
theme_raw: Quaternary faults and folds; M>6 earthquake sources
coverage: United States and offshore-adjacent fault traces
license: Public Domain (U.S. Government work)
formats: KMZ, GIS ZIP, interactive ArcGIS map
access: direct GIS/KMZ download; interactive map
doi_or_citation: USGS Earthquake Hazards Program, Quaternary Fault and Fold Database of the United States
time_span: Quaternary, database definition historically <1.6 Ma; active maintenance through USGS hazard program
notes: USGS retired the old database search on 2026-02-26, but legacy reports remain through the interactive fault map and GIS/KMZ downloads remain linked.
desc_zh: USGS 第四纪断层与褶皱数据库是美国活动断层资料的权威入口，记录有大型地震地表变形证据的断层和相关褶皱。页面提供交互式地图、KMZ 和 GIS ZIP 下载，并连接到历史说明与参考文献。该库直接服务美国国家地震危险性图，是美国断层数据的核心来源。
desc_en: The USGS Quaternary Fault and Fold Database is the authoritative U.S. source for faults and associated folds with geologic evidence of coseismic surface deformation in large earthquakes. The current portal provides an interactive map plus KMZ and GIS ZIP downloads, while legacy descriptive reports remain accessible through the map. It is a primary input lineage for U.S. national seismic hazard modeling.
scale: national hazard mapping; mixed source scales
file_size: 13 MB KMZ; 16 MB GIS ZIP listed by USGS

name: USGS Shapefiles of faults for the United States, Canada, and Australia
provider: U.S. Geological Survey
theme_raw: surface faults; critical minerals evidential layer
license: Public Domain Mark 1.0
formats: ESRI Shapefile
access: ScienceBase/USGS data release
doi_or_citation: McCafferty et al. (2025), USGS data release, https://doi.org/10.5066/P970GDD5
time_span: published 2025; compiled from older regional/geologic sources
notes: This is not an active-fault-only hazard database; it is useful as an open generalized fault layer for North America/Australia mineral prospectivity and structural context.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布提供美国和加拿大合并断层 shapefile 以及澳大利亚断层 shapefile，主要用于沉积型铅锌矿成矿远景评价中的证据图层。它整合了北美地质图、GEM 活动断层和澳大利亚地质数据等来源。该数据更偏一般地质断层背景，不应等同于活动断层清单。
desc_en: This USGS data release provides shapefiles of faults for the United States and Canada, plus a separate fault shapefile for Australia. The layers were assembled as evidential inputs for sediment-hosted Pb-Zn mineral prospectivity modeling and combine several geologic and active-fault sources. They are valuable open structural context layers, but they are not restricted to active seismic faults.

name: Active Faults of Eurasia Database (AFEAD)
provider: Geological Institute, Russian Academy of Sciences; ESSD authors
theme_raw: active faults of Eurasia
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: Shapefile; web map
access: direct sheet/shapefile downloads; web map
doi_or_citation: Zelenin et al. (2022), Earth System Science Data, https://doi.org/10.5194/essd-14-4489-2022
time_span: published 2022; source literature and maps span historical to modern active-tectonic studies
notes: ESSD article reports 48,205 objects in shapefile format at detail sufficient for 1:1,000,000 scale, from 612 sources including many Russian-language and hard-copy references.
desc_zh: AFEAD 是欧亚大陆尺度的高细节活动断层数据库，包含断层迹线、活动可信度、滑动速率等级、运动性质和来源文献等字段。数据以 shapefile 分幅发布，并有在线地图，适合补齐中亚、俄罗斯远东和北亚等全球库常不完整的区域。ESSD 论文说明其由 612 个来源汇编而成。
desc_en: AFEAD is a continental-scale active-fault geodatabase for Eurasia. It provides fault geometry, sense of motion, activity-confidence ranks, slip-rate ranks, references, and supporting text, distributed as shapefiles and a web map. The ESSD data paper reports 48,205 objects compiled from 612 sources, making it especially useful for central, northern, and Russian-language Eurasian fault literature.
source_type: peer-reviewed data paper plus institutional download site
feature_count: 48,205 objects

name: China Active Faults Database (CAFD) and CEFS web system
provider: China Earthquake Administration / active tectonics research team
theme_raw: China active faults; CEFS V2; WMS/WFS
license: custom service/data-access terms; ESSD article text CC-BY-4.0
formats: WebGIS, WMS, WFS, ArcGIS feature services
access: web portal; WMS/WFS service; downloads through platform/client workflows
doi_or_citation: Xu et al. (2024), ESSD; data DOI https://doi.org/10.12031/activefault.china.400.2023.db
time_span: active fault database version described in 2024; integrates surveys from roughly the previous two decades
notes: The ESSD paper states active-fault WMS/WFS services are online and data can be browsed, queried, analyzed, and downloaded through introduced platforms/clients, while related earthquake catalogs are not freely downloadable.
desc_zh: CAFD 是中国及邻区 1:400 万活动断层数据库和 WebGIS 查询系统，整合近二十年区域活动断层调查和较高比例尺资料。论文明确给出 WMS 与 WFS 服务，可用于浏览、查询、GIS 接入和部分下载流程。其开放性比 GitHub/Zenodo 数据弱，需要把服务可达性和批量下载限制分开记录。
desc_en: The China Active Faults Database is a national-scale 1:4,000,000 active-fault database for China and adjacent regions, exposed through the CEFS web GIS system. The ESSD paper documents ArcGIS-based WMS and WFS services for browsing, querying, analysis, GIS integration, and platform/client download workflows. It is a key official source, but its bulk-download openness is more constrained than repository-hosted datasets.
access_status: public web service; bulk download constraints noted
source_type: peer-reviewed data paper plus national WebGIS service

name: New Zealand Active Faults Database (NZAFD)
provider: GNS Science / Earth Sciences New Zealand
theme_raw: NZAFD AF250 and high-resolution active fault traces
license: GNS Science copyright with public web/download access
formats: ArcGIS REST, GeoJSON query, PBF, web map, downloadable AF250 layer
access: GNS webmap; ArcGIS REST service; AF250 download
doi_or_citation: NZAFD; see also Langridge et al. and 2024 high-resolution dataset paper
time_span: faults rupturing in last 125,000 years, except Taupō Rift last 25,000 years
notes: GNS distinguishes AF250 nationwide generalized active faults and high-resolution active-fault data available for selected regions through the same service.
desc_zh: NZAFD 汇集新西兰陆上活动断层地表迹线，定义为过去 12.5 万年内发生过地表破裂的断层，陶波裂谷采用 2.5 万年标准。数据库包含全国 1:250,000 概化层和部分地区高分辨率层，字段描述断层名、运动性质、位移、复发间隔等。GNS 提供网页和 ArcGIS REST 服务，可作为新西兰断层权威入口。
desc_en: The New Zealand Active Faults Database stores surface traces of onshore active faults in Aotearoa New Zealand, generally defined by surface rupture in the last 125,000 years, with a 25,000-year criterion in the Taupō Rift. It includes a national AF250 layer and selected high-resolution regional layers with attributes such as name, movement sense, displacement, and recurrence interval. GNS exposes the data through a web map and ArcGIS REST services.
access_status: open service/download for AF250; selected high-resolution layers view/service access
scale: 1:250,000 nationwide plus selected 1:10,000 or better high-resolution areas

name: Active Fault Database of Japan
provider: Geological Survey of Japan, AIST
theme_raw: behavioral segments; active faults in Japan
license: custom non-commercial web-use terms; citation required
formats: web map/database; Google Maps interface
access: interactive web database
doi_or_citation: AIST Active Fault Database of Japan; cite Active Fault Research Center / GSJ AIST
time_span: active faults subdivided by behavioral segments; parameters from paleoseismic and geologic literature
notes: AIST notice says displayed active fault data cannot be used for commercial purpose; proper source citation is required for other uses.
desc_zh: 日本 AIST 活动断层数据库按 behavioral segment 组织日本活动断层，提供断层几何、地质和古地震参数、滑动速率、单次位移、复发间隔和未来破裂概率等信息。数据来自论文、学位论文和调查资料汇编，适合做日本活动断层的权威查询入口。开放性主要是网页查询，使用条款限制商业用途。
desc_en: The AIST Active Fault Database of Japan organizes Japanese active faults by behavioral segments and provides geologic and paleoseismic parameters such as slip rate, displacement per event, recurrence interval, and rupture probability. The database is compiled from journal articles, theses, and other documents and is a key national reference for Japanese active-fault information. Access is primarily through the web map/database and is subject to non-commercial and citation requirements.
access_status: public web database; non-commercial use constraint
scale: behavioral segment database; mixed detailed source scales
feature_count: hundreds of behavioral segments

name: Geoscience Australia Neotectonic Features Database
provider: Geoscience Australia
theme_raw: faults, folds, neotectonic features
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: KML, CSV through tool, web mapping application
access: web application; metadata catalogue; KML extract
doi_or_citation: Geoscience Australia PID https://pid.geoscience.gov.au/dataset/ga/74056
time_span: Neotectonic Era, roughly past 5-10 million years
notes: Metadata states the tool supports search/exploration, reports, and download of feature data and geometries as CSV or KML; catalogue also links a 2019 KML extract.
desc_zh: 澳大利亚新构造特征数据库记录与新近构造地震活动相关的断层、褶皱和其他构造特征，时间范围约为过去 500-1000 万年。Geoscience Australia 提供在线检索、报告生成以及 CSV/KML 几何下载，并以 CC BY 4.0 发布。该库是澳大利亚活动/潜在活动构造特征的重要官方来源。
desc_en: Geoscience Australia's Neotectonic Features Database documents faults, folds, and related features believed to relate to large earthquakes during the Neotectonic Era, roughly the past 5-10 million years. The catalogue and mapping tool support feature exploration, reports, and geometry/data export as CSV or KML, with CC BY 4.0 licensing. It is the key official Australian neotectonic-fault source.
source_type: official government catalogue and web application
feature_count: over 300 features noted in factsheet lineage
file_size: 2019 KML extract 4.5 MB

name: Fault Activity Map of California (2010)
provider: California Geological Survey
theme_raw: California fault activity map
license: public state open data terms
formats: ArcGIS Hub feature layer, downloadable open-data formats
access: California state geoportal / ArcGIS Hub
doi_or_citation: Jennings, C.W. and Bryant, W.A. (2010), Fault Activity Map of California, CGS Geologic Data Map GDM-6
time_span: fault activity classes from Holocene to older Quaternary/pre-Quaternary compilation
notes: Digital database for faults shown on the 2010 Fault Activity Map of California at 1:750,000 scale.
desc_zh: 加州断层活动图是 California Geological Survey 发布的 2010 年州级断层活动数字数据库，对加州断层按活动时代和性质进行汇编。数据通过 California State Geoportal/ArcGIS Hub 提供，可下载并用于 GIS。它适合与 USGS QFFDB、UCERF 和地方断层数据交叉核对。
desc_en: The Fault Activity Map of California is the California Geological Survey's 2010 digital database for faults shown on the statewide map by Jennings and Bryant. It classifies California faults by activity and provides GIS-ready access through the California State Geoportal and ArcGIS Hub. It is a useful authoritative state layer to compare with USGS QFFDB, UCERF, and local fault datasets.
source_type: official state geologic survey open-data layer

name: Database of Individual Seismogenic Sources (DISS) v3.3.1
provider: Istituto Nazionale di Geofisica e Vulcanologia (INGV)
theme_raw: individual seismogenic sources; DISS
license: CC-BY-4.0 except where otherwise noted
formats: browser database, WFS, WMS, GIS downloads
access: web portal; OGC WFS/WMS; downloads
doi_or_citation: DISS Working Group (2025), Version 3.3.1, https://doi.org/10.13127/diss3.3.1
time_span: potential sources for earthquakes larger than M 5.5; version 3.3.1 cited 2025
notes: DISS is a georeferenced repository for tectonic, fault, and paleoseismological information designed for earthquake hazard applications.
desc_zh: DISS 是意大利及邻区潜在 M>5.5 地震源的权威数据库，包含独立地震源、复合源和相关构造/古地震信息。v3.3.1 提供浏览器、OGC WFS/WMS 和 GIS 访问，并采用 CC BY 4.0 条款。它是欧洲和地中海地震源建模的重要基础库。
desc_en: DISS v3.3.1 is INGV's georeferenced database of potential sources for earthquakes larger than M 5.5 in Italy and surrounding areas. It stores tectonic, fault, and paleoseismological information for hazard applications and exposes data through browser tools, OGC WFS/WMS services, and GIS downloads. The service page cites CC BY 4.0 licensing except where otherwise noted.
scale: seismogenic source model; mixed source scales

name: ITHACA - ITaly HAzards from CApable faulting
provider: ISPRA
theme_raw: active capable faults of Italy
license: custom ISPRA/data.europa.eu public service terms
formats: view service, web GIS
access: web service and portal view access
doi_or_citation: ITHACA Working Group / ISPRA, Italy Hazards from Capable Faulting
time_span: capable faults with potential surface deformation; periodically updated
notes: The data.europa.eu record documents a view service for capable faults; download openness still needs deeper service inspection.
desc_zh: ITHACA 是 ISPRA 维护的意大利活动能动断层数据库，重点收集可能在地表或近地表产生位移的 capable faults。该库与地表破裂危险性、土地利用规划和地震危险性评估密切相关。当前公开线索包括 ISPRA 项目页和 data.europa.eu 视图服务，后续需进一步解析服务端点和批量下载方式。
desc_en: ITHACA is ISPRA's Italian database of active capable faults, focusing on tectonic structures that can produce displacement at or near the ground surface. It is directly relevant to surface-faulting hazard, land-use planning, and seismic hazard assessment. Public evidence includes the ISPRA project page and European data-portal view services; the next phase should inspect service endpoints for bulk-download options.
access_status: public view service; download route needs deeper inspection

name: European Database of Seismogenic Faults 2013 (EDSF13)
provider: EFEHR / SHARE / EPOS seismology services
theme_raw: European seismogenic faults; SHARE active faults
license: custom EFEHR/EPOS service terms
formats: EPRI vector file format; web services/portal
access: EFEHR/seismofaults portal download/service
doi_or_citation: Basili et al. (2013) / SHARE European Database of Seismogenic Faults
time_span: 2013 SHARE hazard-model active fault input
notes: EFEHR documentation states EDSF is a compilation of fault sources and provides data files in EPRI vector format.
desc_zh: EDSF13 是 SHARE 欧洲地震危险性模型使用的欧洲地震源断层汇编，包含复合地震源、背景区和面积源等建模要素。EFEHR 文档说明其提供 EPRI vector 格式的数据文件，是 EFSM20 之前欧洲统一断层源建模的重要版本。它更偏地震源模型而非纯地质线迹图层。
desc_en: EDSF13 is the European Database of Seismogenic Faults compiled for the SHARE seismic hazard model. EFEHR documentation describes it as a compilation of fault sources with data files provided in EPRI vector format, alongside related background and area sources. It is an important historical European source model, distinct from a simple surface-trace geology layer.
feature_count: about 988 fully parameterized records noted by SHARE documentation

name: European Fault-Source Model 2020 (EFSM20)
provider: EFEHR / ESHM20 / seismofaults.eu authors
theme_raw: European fault-source model 2020
license: openly accessible and reusable per NHESS data paper; service terms to verify
formats: open-standard fault-source model formats; seismofaults portal
access: seismofaults.eu portal; paper-backed data/service
doi_or_citation: Basili et al. (2024), The European Fault-Source Model 2020, NHESS, https://doi.org/10.5194/nhess-24-3945-2024
time_span: 2020 model for European Seismic Hazard Model 2020
notes: NHESS paper reports 1,248 crustal faults and four subduction systems, openly accessible and reusable in recognized open-standard formats.
desc_zh: EFSM20 是欧洲 2020 地震危险性模型的主要断层源输入数据，覆盖欧洲大陆及周边俯冲系统。论文说明其包含 1,248 条地壳断层和 4 个俯冲系统，并采用开放标准格式公开复用。它比 EDSF13 更新，更适合欧洲区域地震源建模和跨国断层源比较。
desc_en: EFSM20 is the geologic fault-source input dataset for the European Seismic Hazard Model 2020. The NHESS data paper reports 1,248 crustal faults and four subduction systems, with open-standard formats and open reusability. It is the modern successor-scale European model for cross-border fault-source comparison and seismic hazard applications.
source_type: peer-reviewed model/data paper and regional web portal
feature_count: 1,248 crustal faults and 4 subduction systems

name: Quaternary Active Faults Database of Iberia (QAFI v3)
provider: IGME / QAFI Working Group
theme_raw: QAFI; Iberian Quaternary active faults
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
formats: web database; downloadable QAFI data
access: web portal; downloadable database
doi_or_citation: García-Mayordomo et al. (2012), Journal of Iberian Geology; QAFI v3 portal
time_span: Quaternary, last 2.6 million years
notes: QAFI portal states v3 is for free use under CC BY-SA 4.0; earlier paper and NHESS preprint state QAFI can be downloaded from the IGME website.
desc_zh: QAFI 是伊比利亚半岛第四纪活动断层数据库，收录过去 260 万年有活动证据的断层并服务断层相关地震危险性评估。v3 门户明确采用 CC BY-SA 4.0 免费使用许可，并提供在线记录和下载线索。该库覆盖西班牙与葡萄牙，是欧洲南西部区域断层源的重要补充。
desc_en: QAFI is the Quaternary Active Faults Database of Iberia, documenting faults in Spain and Portugal with evidence of activity during the last 2.6 million years. The v3 portal states free use under CC BY-SA 4.0 and provides web records and download access. It is a key regional complement to broader European fault-source models.
feature_count: 299 records reported for v3 in NHESS preprint

name: Greek Database of Seismogenic Sources (GreDaSS)
provider: University of Ferrara / Aristotle University of Thessaloniki and collaborators
theme_raw: Greek seismogenic sources; Aegean active faults
license: custom public web portal terms
formats: web portal/database
access: public web portal
doi_or_citation: Caputo et al. / GreDaSS publications; Annals of Geophysics article
time_span: active-fault and seismogenic-source compilation
notes: GreDaSS is a repository of geological, tectonic, and active-fault data for Greek territory and nearby countries; download API needs further inspection.
desc_zh: GreDaSS 是希腊及周边爱琴海区域地震源数据库，收集地质、构造和活动断层信息，用于地震情景、危险性评估和活动变形研究。它提供公共门户和源参数查询，是较早的希腊区域地震源系统。当前 v001 先记录门户级可访问性，后续需解析是否有批量 GIS 下载。
desc_en: GreDaSS is a database of seismogenic sources for Greece and the broader Aegean region, compiling geological, tectonic, and active-fault information for earthquake scenarios, hazard assessment, and active deformation studies. It is a public portal and an important regional source model. This first pass records portal-level access; bulk GIS export should be inspected in a later phase.
access_status: public portal; bulk download needs inspection

name: Hellenic DataBase of Active Faults (HeDBAF)
provider: Hellenic Survey of Geology & Mineral Exploration / EPPO and Greek research institutes
theme_raw: Hellenic active faults; HeDBAF
license: custom public web portal terms
formats: web GIS/database
access: public web portal
doi_or_citation: Galanakis et al. (2025), EGU abstract on HeDBAF
time_span: national active-fault database started 2021; ongoing
notes: HeDBAF is an official national collaboration under EPPO; useful links page is also a strong lead list for regional/national fault databases.
desc_zh: HeDBAF 是希腊国家级活动断层数据库，由相关高校、研究机构和地质调查机构在 EPPO 组织下共同建设。它面向更广泛的希腊/爱琴海区域活动断层参数和描述信息整理，并提供网页访问。其门户的“useful links”还可作为下一轮追踪欧洲、土耳其、台湾、新西兰等断层库的线索节点。
desc_en: HeDBAF is an official Hellenic active-fault database developed collaboratively by Greek geological, academic, and research institutions under EPPO. It organizes parametric and descriptive active-fault information for the broader Greek and Aegean region and is available through a public web portal. Its useful-links page is also an excellent lead hub for additional regional and national fault databases.
access_status: public portal; bulk download needs inspection

name: AFG - Active Faults Greece 1:25,000 database
provider: GFZ Data Services; National Observatory of Athens and collaborators
theme_raw: Active Faults Greece; geomorphology-based fault traces
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: download archive, ArcGIS FeatureServer, layer files, web map
access: GFZ data archive; ArcGIS REST FeatureServer; web map
doi_or_citation: Begg et al. (2025), GFZ Data Services, https://doi.org/10.5880/fidgeo.2025.047
time_span: released 2025; includes active, probably-active, and historic surface-rupture traces
notes: GFZ page states CC BY 4.0 and lists archive, web map, FeatureServer and layer-file access. ArcGIS FeatureServer layer AFG_v1_2025 returned 3,814 features during v004 verification; GFZ abstract reports 3,815 traces assigned to 892 interpreted faults.
desc_zh: AFG 是 2025 年发布的希腊 1:25,000 地貌解释活动断层数据库，记录 3,815 条断层迹线并归并为 892 条解释断层。v004 复核确认其 GFZ 归档、ArcGIS FeatureServer、网页地图和 QGIS/ArcGIS 图层文件均有稳定入口，FeatureServer 查询返回 3,814 条要素。它是当前希腊全国尺度最细的开放活动断层线索之一。
desc_en: AFG is a 2025 geomorphology-based 1:25,000 active-fault database for Greece, with 3,815 reported traces assigned to 892 interpreted faults. The v004 check confirmed stable GFZ archive, ArcGIS FeatureServer, web-map, and GIS layer-file routes, with the FeatureServer returning 3,814 features. It remains one of the strongest current open national active-fault resources for Greece.
source_type: DOI data repository and web service
feature_count: GFZ reports 3,815 traces and 892 interpreted faults; FeatureServer count returned 3,814 features in v004

name: NOAfaults KMZ layer
provider: National Observatory of Athens
theme_raw: NOAfaults Greece active fault KMZ
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: KMZ, GMT, PNG map, revision-history PDF, release PDF
access: Zenodo record download; DOI repository files
doi_or_citation: Ganas, Oikonomou and Tsimi (2013), NOAfaults; Zenodo record 16728922
time_span: NOAFAULTS Version 7.0, Zenodo publication 2025; focal mechanisms updated to June 2025 per record
notes: Version 7.0 adds new thematic layers including sampling sites, NOA Surface Rupture Database, Cyprus faults, updated focal mechanisms, and the 2020 Samos rupture plane. Zenodo record states 3,441 active faults and CC BY 4.0 license.
desc_zh: NOAFAULTS v7.0 是希腊国家天文台维护的活动断层 KMZ/GMT 数据集，Zenodo 记录提供 KMZ、GMT、地图和版本说明下载。v7.0 记录 3,441 条活动断层，并新增采样点、NOA 地表破裂数据库、塞浦路斯断层、2020 Samos 破裂面和更新的震源机制等图层。它与 AFG 的地貌制图方法不同，适合做希腊/爱琴海活动断层多源对照。
desc_en: NOAFAULTS v7.0 is a National Observatory of Athens active-fault dataset distributed through Zenodo as KMZ, GMT, map, and release-history files. The v7.0 record reports 3,441 active faults and adds sampling sites, the NOA Surface Rupture Database, Cyprus faults, the 2020 Samos rupture plane, and updated focal-mechanism layers. It remains methodologically distinct from AFG and is useful for multi-source Greek and Aegean active-fault comparison.
access_status: open repository download; license needs file-level check
feature_count: 3,441 active faults reported in Zenodo v7.0 record; slip-rate data for 164 faults
file_size: NOAFaults_v7.0.kmz 871.5 kB; GMT 1.38 MB listed by Zenodo API

name: Database of active and potentially-active continental faults in Chile at 1:25,000 scale
provider: PANGAEA; Melnick et al.
theme_raw: Chile active and potentially active continental faults
license: PANGAEA open data terms / dataset license to verify
formats: ESRI Shapefile, KMZ, Excel spreadsheet
access: PANGAEA DOI landing page and file download
doi_or_citation: Melnick et al. (2020), https://doi.org/10.1594/PANGAEA.922241
time_span: active and potentially active continental faults; mapped at 1:25,000
notes: PANGAEA abstract reports 959 fault strands grouped into 17 fault systems, classified as proved, probable, or possible activity.
desc_zh: 该 PANGAEA 数据集是智利首个综合活动及潜在活动大陆断层数据库，基于已有研究和 1:25,000 新制图，结合地质、地貌、TanDEM-X 与 LiDAR 资料。数据包含 959 条断层段，归并为 17 个断层系统，并按活动证据分为确定、可能和潜在。文件提供 shapefile、KMZ 和 Excel。
desc_en: This PANGAEA dataset is a comprehensive database of active and potentially active continental faults in Chile, based on prior studies and new 1:25,000 mapping using geologic, geomorphic, TanDEM-X, and LiDAR evidence. It includes 959 fault strands grouped into 17 fault systems and classified by activity confidence. Data are provided as shapefiles, KMZ, and Excel spreadsheets.
feature_count: 959 fault strands; 17 fault systems

name: Active Tectonics of the Andes (ATA v1.0)
provider: Veloza, Styron, Taylor and Mora
theme_raw: open-source active faults for northwest South America
license: open-source archive; specific license to verify
formats: multiple digital formats
access: article/project archive download links
doi_or_citation: Veloza et al. (2012), GSA Today, https://doi.org/10.1130/GSAT-G156A.1
time_span: active structures compiled through 2012 publication
notes: GSA Today page describes ATA v1.0 as a digital archive of more than 400 active faults available in a variety of digital formats.
desc_zh: ATA v1.0 是北安第斯/南美西北部活动构造开放档案，基于文献、野外制图、DEM 解译、地震和 GPS 速度场整理。它包含 400 多条活动断层或活动构造，曾作为 GEM 全球活动断层库的区域来源之一。后续需要追踪项目下载镜像和文件级许可证。
desc_en: Active Tectonics of the Andes v1.0 is an open-source archive of active structures for the northern Andes, compiled from literature, field mapping, DEM interpretation, earthquakes, and GPS velocity fields. The GSA Today article describes more than 400 active faults available in multiple digital formats. It is also one of the regional sources feeding broader GEM active-fault compilations.
access_status: open archive lead; direct file URLs need follow-up
feature_count: more than 400 active faults

name: SARA Active Faults Database
provider: Global Earthquake Model Foundation and South American Risk Assessment collaborators
theme_raw: South American Risk Assessment active faults
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
formats: GitHub-hosted geospatial files
access: GitHub repository
doi_or_citation: Alvarado et al. (2017), DOI:10.13117/SARA-ACTIVE-FAULTS
time_span: SARA project regional active-fault compilation
notes: Listed by GEM GAF-DB README as a constituent dataset; separate repository helps preserve regional provenance.
desc_zh: SARA Active Faults 是 GEM 与南美风险评估项目协作形成的南美活动断层数据仓库。它是 GEM 全球活动断层库的区域来源之一，但独立仓库有助于保留区域版本和引用线索。数据通过 GitHub 开放，许可为 CC BY-SA 4.0。
desc_en: SARA Active Faults is a South American active-fault database produced by GEM and collaborators in the South American Risk Assessment project. It is listed as a constituent dataset of the GEM Global Active Faults Database, but the standalone repository is important for regional provenance and citation. The data are hosted on GitHub under CC BY-SA 4.0.

name: North Africa Active Faults
provider: Global Earthquake Model Foundation contributors
theme_raw: North Africa active fault traces
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: GitHub-hosted geospatial files
access: GitHub repository
doi_or_citation: https://doi.org/10.13117/n-africa-active-faults
time_span: work-in-progress GEM regional compilation
notes: Repository README states the database is compiled from published sources and public GIS data for GEM Global Active Faults.
desc_zh: North Africa Active Faults 是 GEM 全球活动断层计划的北非区域断层迹线数据仓库，由已发表资料和公共 GIS 数据汇编。它提供区域级开放下载和 DOI 线索，适合补足全球库在北非的来源追踪。仓库说明仍为工作中状态，使用时应保留版本和质量说明。
desc_en: North Africa Active Faults is a GEM regional repository of active fault traces in North Africa, compiled from published sources and public GIS data for the Global Active Faults effort. It provides open GitHub access and a DOI lead, making it useful for provenance-aware regional extraction. The repository notes that it is work in progress, so versioning and quality caveats should be retained.

name: Active Faults of Central America and the Caribbean
provider: Global Earthquake Model Foundation / CCARA contributors
theme_raw: Central America and Caribbean active faults; CCAF
license: open GitHub repository license to verify
formats: GitHub-hosted geospatial files
access: GitHub repository
doi_or_citation: https://doi.org/10.13117/central-america-caribbean-active-faults
time_span: CCARA/GEM regional compilation
notes: Repository describes a database of active faults for seismic hazard analysis, earth science research, and education.
desc_zh: 该仓库汇集中美洲和加勒比地区活动断层，面向地震危险性分析、地球科学研究和教育使用。它填补 GEM 全球活动断层计划在该区域的重要空白，并提供 DOI 线索。后续需核实仓库内具体格式和许可证文件。
desc_en: This repository compiles active faults for Central America and the Caribbean for seismic hazard analysis, earth science research, and education. It fills an important regional gap in the GEM Global Active Faults effort and provides a DOI lead. A later pass should inspect the repository files to confirm exact formats and license text.

name: Malawi Active Fault Database (MAFD)
provider: Williams, Wedmore and collaborators; Zenodo
theme_raw: Malawi onshore-offshore active faults
license: Zenodo open record; file license to verify
formats: geospatial database files via Zenodo
access: Zenodo DOI download
doi_or_citation: Williams et al. (2022), The Malawi Active Fault Database, https://doi.org/10.1029/2022GC010425; data DOI https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.5507190
time_span: active fault traces in Malawi Rift region
notes: Article abstract describes an open-access geospatial database of 113-114 active fault traces depending on version wording.
desc_zh: MAFD 是马拉维及邻近坦桑尼亚、莫桑比克地区的陆上-近海活动断层地理数据库，记录约 113-114 条活动断层迹线。该数据支撑马拉维裂谷地震危险性和构造演化研究，并通过 Zenodo DOI 开放。它是东非裂谷西支少数据区的重要开放断层库。
desc_en: The Malawi Active Fault Database is an onshore-offshore geospatial database of active fault traces in Malawi and neighboring Tanzania and Mozambique. The associated paper describes roughly 113-114 active fault traces and frames the dataset for regional seismic hazard and tectonic evolution studies. It is one of the key open active-fault resources for a data-sparse part of the western East African Rift.
access_status: open repository download; file license needs check
source_type: peer-reviewed article and Zenodo data repository
feature_count: about 113-114 fault traces

name: Malawi Seismogenic Source Model (MSSM)
provider: Williams, Wedmore and collaborators; Zenodo
theme_raw: Malawi seismogenic source model
license: Zenodo open record; file license to verify
formats: geospatial database components via Zenodo
access: Zenodo DOI download
doi_or_citation: Williams et al. (2022), NHESS, https://doi.org/10.5194/nhess-22-3607-2022; data DOI https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.5599616
time_span: published model versions 2021-2022
notes: MSSM adapts faults from MAFD into section, fault, and multi-fault seismogenic source representations with magnitude/frequency parameters.
desc_zh: MSSM 是基于马拉维活动断层库发展出的地震源模型，记录活动断层的几何、滑动速率和震源属性，并把要素分为 section、fault 和 multi-fault 类型。它适合从简单断层迹线扩展到地震危险性模型输入。该数据通过 Zenodo 开放，但文件级许可证仍需下一轮核实。
desc_en: The Malawi Seismogenic Source Model adapts the Malawi Active Fault Database into a geospatial source model with geometry, slip rate, and seismogenic properties such as earthquake magnitude and frequency. Features are organized as sections, faults, and multi-fault sources, making it useful for moving from traces to hazard-model inputs. The data are available through Zenodo, with file-level licensing to verify.
access_status: open repository download; file license needs check

name: Taiwan Active Fault Distribution Map (1:500,000 Scale Value File)
provider: Ministry of Economic Affairs / Geological Survey and Mining Management Agency, Taiwan
theme_raw: Taiwan active fault distribution map; WMS
license: Taiwan government open data terms
formats: CSV value file with WMS layer URL; GIS WMS
access: Taiwan data.gov.tw open data download; WMS
doi_or_citation: Taiwan Active Fault Distribution Map, Geological Survey and Mining Management Agency
time_span: active fault map current public open-data record
notes: Open-data page states the resource provides a WMS service URL for GIS software such as QGIS; related official active-fault map versions should be cross-checked.
desc_zh: 台湾活动断层分布图开放资料提供 1:500,000 比例尺断层图层的 WMS 使用线索，用户可下载值文件并在 QGIS 等 GIS 软件中加载服务。该数据由台湾官方地质/矿业主管机构发布，是台湾活动断层空间分布的官方入口之一。后续应继续追踪更高比例尺版本和 TEM 地震源数据的可下载附件。
desc_en: Taiwan's Active Fault Distribution Map open-data record provides a 1:500,000-scale active-fault layer through a downloadable value file containing a WMS service URL for GIS clients such as QGIS. It is an official government entry point for Taiwan active-fault spatial data. A later pass should trace higher-resolution map versions and Taiwan Earthquake Model source-database supplements.

name: EMME14 Active Faults Database and Fault Section Model
provider: Earthquake Model of the Middle East (EMME); EFEHR/GEM/ETH partners
theme_raw: Middle East active fault sections; EMME14 fault section database
coverage: Middle East region from Turkey to Pakistan and surrounding countries
license: custom EFEHR/GEM model terms
formats: web documentation; hazard computation input package; OpenQuake/NRML source model files; fault section database
access: EFEHR documentation and downloadable hazard input package/model files
doi_or_citation: Danciu et al. / Şeşetyan et al. (2018), The 2014 Earthquake Model of the Middle East: seismogenic sources, Bulletin of Earthquake Engineering, doi:10.1007/s10518-017-0096-8
time_span: EMME14 regional model; fault section database compiled for 2014 hazard model
notes: EFEHR documentation reports 3,397 active fault sections and about 91,551 km of parameterized faults; not all compiled faults were used in the final fault source model. The hazard computation input page states the package includes point, area, simple and complex fault source representations.
desc_zh: EMME14 活动断层数据库是中东地震模型的断层分段资料，覆盖土耳其至巴基斯坦一带的跨国地震构造区域。EFEHR 文档说明其包含 3,397 个活动断层段、总长约 91,551 km，并为断层源模型和 OpenQuake 危险性计算输入提供基础。该条适合作为中东区域断层源骨架，但需要在后续解析具体下载包和服务端证书状态。
desc_en: The EMME14 Active Faults Database is the fault-section component of the Earthquake Model of the Middle East, covering a transnational region from Turkey toward Pakistan. EFEHR documentation reports 3,397 active fault sections and about 91,551 km of parameterized faults, with a subset used in the fault-source model and OpenQuake hazard inputs. It is a key regional fault-source backbone, although the exact downloadable package and service stability need continued checking.
access_status: public documentation and model-input download route; service stability needs verification
feature_count: 3,397 fault segments; 91,551 km reported by EFEHR

name: J-SHIS Seismic Source Fault Data
provider: National Research Institute for Earth Science and Disaster Resilience (NIED), Japan
theme_raw: Japan seismic source faults; J-SHIS source model
license: custom J-SHIS/NIED terms
formats: downloadable data files; web map; source fault layers
access: J-SHIS download page and map interface
doi_or_citation: J-SHIS / National Seismic Hazard Maps for Japan; Fujiwara et al. related documentation
time_span: national seismic hazard map source-fault models; updated through J-SHIS releases
notes: J-SHIS download page lists seismic source fault data among downloadable probabilistic seismic hazard map data. This complements AIST active-fault behavioral segments with source-fault model inputs.
desc_zh: J-SHIS 是日本防灾科研机构 NIED 的地震危险性信息站，除危险性图结果外，也提供制作危险性图所需的源模型、地震活动和地下结构等资料。本条记录其可下载的 seismic source fault 数据，作为日本地震源断层模型，与 AIST 活动断层数据库形成互补。后续应继续核对具体下载包版本和 J-SHIS 使用条款。
desc_en: J-SHIS, operated by NIED, provides not only probabilistic seismic hazard map outputs but also input data such as seismic source models, seismicity, underground structure, and source-fault layers. This row records the downloadable seismic source fault data as a Japan source-fault model complementing the AIST active-fault behavioral-segment database. Later work should inspect package versions and detailed J-SHIS use terms.

name: Taiwan Earthquake Model On-land Seismogenic Structure Source Database
provider: Taiwan Earthquake Model (TEM) project; National Taiwan University and collaborators
theme_raw: Taiwan on-land seismogenic structures; TEM source database
license: article/supplement terms to verify
formats: paper supplementary archive; tabular/geospatial structural model data
access: TAO article page and supplementary file download
doi_or_citation: Shyu et al. (2016), A New On-Land Seismogenic Structure Source Database from the Taiwan Earthquake Model (TEM) Project, TAO, doi:10.3319/TAO.2015.11.27.02(TEM)
time_span: first TEM on-land source database published 2016; update paper in 2020 reports 45 structures
notes: Search result and PDF text indicate detailed datasets are included in a supplementary file; 2020 update reports 45 structures including 7 newly added ones. Keep separate from the official 1:500,000 active fault WMS because TEM is a 3-D seismogenic source database.
desc_zh: TEM 陆上地震构造源数据库是台湾地震模型项目为地震危险性评估建立的三维 seismogenic structure source 数据集。2016 年论文说明详细数据包含在补充文件中，2020 年更新论文将结构数量扩展到 45 个、包括 7 个新增结构。它与官方活动断层分布图不同，更适合断层面距离、震源几何和 PSHA 模型输入。
desc_en: The Taiwan Earthquake Model on-land seismogenic structure source database is a 3-D source-structure dataset developed for Taiwan seismic hazard assessment. The 2016 paper states that detailed datasets are included as supplementary material, and the 2020 update reports 45 structures including 7 additions. It is distinct from the official active-fault map because it targets source geometry, distance-to-fault-plane constraints, and PSHA inputs.
access_status: paper supplementary download; file-level license needs verification
feature_count: 38 structures in first version; 45 structures reported in 2020 update

name: GEM Northeastern Asia Active Faults
provider: Global Earthquake Model Foundation contributors
theme_raw: Northeastern Asia active fault traces
license: open GitHub repository; license file to verify
formats: GeoJSON, GeoPackage, GMT, KML, ESRI Shapefile
access: GitHub repository
doi_or_citation: https://doi.org/10.13117/ne-asia-active-faults; GEM NEA model documentation
time_span: work-in-progress GEM regional compilation used for Northeast Asia hazard modeling
notes: Repository includes geojson, geopackage, gmt, kml, and shapefile folders. GEM NEA documentation says a new shallow active-fault dataset contains roughly 291 active fault traces.
desc_zh: GEM Northeastern Asia Active Faults 是 GEM 面向东北亚地震危险性建模整理的区域活动断层仓库。GitHub 仓库提供 GeoJSON、GeoPackage、KML、GMT 和 Shapefile 等格式，GEM NEA 文档称其浅部活动断层数据约含 291 条活动断层迹线。它可作为 GEM 全球库之外的东北亚区域来源追踪。
desc_en: GEM Northeastern Asia Active Faults is a regional active-fault repository assembled for GEM's Northeast Asia hazard model. The GitHub repository exposes GeoJSON, GeoPackage, KML, GMT, and shapefile folders, while GEM NEA documentation describes roughly 291 shallow active fault traces. It is useful for preserving regional provenance outside the homogenized global GEM layer.
feature_count: ~291 active fault traces per GEM NEA documentation

name: HimaTibetMap active structures database
provider: HimaTibetMap project; Taylor, Yin, Styron, Okoronkwo and collaborators
theme_raw: Indo-Asian collision active faults and associated features
coverage: Indo-Asian collision zone, from Iran to Myanmar and India to Siberia
license: open-source GitHub database; license file to verify
formats: Shapefile, KML, GMT, GeoJSON
access: GitHub repository and releases
doi_or_citation: Styron, Taylor and Okoronkwo (2010), HimaTibetMap-1.0; Taylor and Yin Geosphere supplemental material, doi:10.1130/GES00217.1
time_span: Indo-Asian active structures compiled through HimaTibetMap releases
notes: Repository README says the database is open-source and available as .shp, .kml, .gmt, and .geojson; version 1.1 announcement reports over 1000 structures in the Indo-Asian collision zone.
desc_zh: HimaTibetMap 是印度-亚洲碰撞带的开源活动构造数据库，包含活动断层以及缝合带、新生代火山岩等相关要素。仓库提供 Shapefile、KML、GMT 和 GeoJSON，其中 Shapefile 含最多属性；区域范围从伊朗到缅甸、从印度到西伯利亚。该库是喜马拉雅-青藏及周缘活动构造研究的经典开放来源。
desc_en: HimaTibetMap is an open-source database of active faults and related structures in the Indo-Asian collision zone, including suture zones and Cenozoic volcanic rocks. The repository distributes shapefile, KML, GMT, and GeoJSON versions, with the shapefile carrying the richest metadata. It is a classic open source for Himalayan-Tibetan and surrounding active-tectonic mapping.
source_type: scientific GitHub repository and publication supplement
feature_count: over 1000 structures reported for v1.1 announcement

name: The Tien Shan Active Fault Database
provider: COMET / University of Oxford / USGS / GEM collaborators; Zenodo
theme_raw: Tien Shan active faults; Central Asia fault database
license: Zenodo open dataset; file license to verify
formats: GeoPackage, ESRI Shapefile, KMZ; interactive map; GitHub mirror
access: Zenodo DOI record download; COMET map; GitHub mirror
doi_or_citation: King et al. (2024), The Tien Shan Active Fault Database, Zenodo, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.10659677
time_span: published 2024, version 1.0; integrates decades of mapping and field studies
notes: Zenodo record provides three database levels: fine-scale scarp/rupture mapping (~1:10k), regional active fault traces (~1:50k), and seismic-source faults. Files include GPKG, KMZ, and shapefile ZIP.
desc_zh: Tien Shan Active Fault Database 是中亚天山地区的同行评议数字活动断层集合，整合数十年的野外制图、遥感解译和地震破裂研究。数据分三层：约 1:10k 的断层崖/破裂细节、约 1:50k 的区域活动断层迹线，以及可用于震源/形变建模的主要 seismic source faults。Zenodo 提供 GPKG、Shapefile 和 KMZ 下载。
desc_en: The Tien Shan Active Fault Database is a peer-reviewed digital active-fault collection for Central Asia, integrating decades of mapping and field studies. It is organized into three levels: fine-scale scarps and ruptures at about 1:10k, regional active fault traces at about 1:50k, and expert-derived seismic-source faults for geodetic and seismic hazard modeling. Zenodo provides GeoPackage, shapefile, and KMZ downloads.
feature_count: three vector layers/database levels
file_size: 4.6 MB total listed by Zenodo

name: Luangwa Rift Active Fault Database (LRAFD) v1.0
provider: University of Bristol / University of Zambia / Zenodo
theme_raw: Luangwa Rift active fault traces
license: open-source Zenodo/GitHub record; file license to verify
formats: GeoJSON, ESRI Shapefile, KML, GMT, GeoPackage
access: Zenodo DOI record download; GitHub release
doi_or_citation: Turner et al. (2022), Solid Earth, https://doi.org/10.5194/se-13-1731-2022; data DOI https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6513691
time_span: published 2022, v1.0; Quaternary-active fault traces
notes: Zenodo record describes LRAFD as a freely available open-source geospatial database; file package is 70.4 kB and associated GitHub release is listed.
desc_zh: LRAFD 是赞比亚 Luangwa Rift 的开放活动断层迹线数据库，按 GEM 活动断层属性标准设计。每条断层作为连续 GIS 要素记录，并包含断层名、倾向、运动性质、长度、地貌表达、制图方法、活动可信度、暴露质量和参考文献等属性。Zenodo 提供压缩包下载，GitHub 保存开放版本。
desc_en: The Luangwa Rift Active Fault Database is an open-source geospatial database of active fault traces in the Luangwa Rift, Zambia. It follows GEM-style attributes, mapping each fault as a continuous GIS feature with metadata such as dip direction, slip type, length, geomorphic expression, mapping method, activity confidence, exposure quality, and references. Zenodo provides a small downloadable package and links the associated GitHub release.
source_type: DOI research data repository and GitHub release
feature_count: 18 faults identified in paper abstract/snippet
file_size: 70.4 kB package listed by Zenodo

name: Philippines GeoHazards Data - Distribution of Active Faults
provider: PHIVOLCS-DOST via Humanitarian Data Exchange (HDX)
theme_raw: Distribution of Active Faults in the Philippines
license: HDX/open humanitarian data terms; PHIVOLCS source constraints to verify
formats: KMZ, ArcGIS REST MapServer/query GeoJSON
access: HDX KMZ download; official PHIVOLCS ArcGIS REST MapServer service
doi_or_citation: PHIVOLCS-DOST GeoHazards Data / Distribution of Active Faults in the Philippines, March 2019 KMZ
time_span: active fault distribution KMZ, March 2019 resource noted by HDX
notes: HDX page lists a KMZ resource named Distribution of Active Faults in the Philippines_Mar2019.kmz. The official PHIVOLCS ActiveFault ArcGIS MapServer exposes an Active Fault polyline layer with JSON/GeoJSON query support, although returnCountOnly is not supported by the service. Lychee cannot reliably validate PHIVOLCS HTTPS endpoints in v003 (connection reset/invalid response), so HDX remains the row URL while PHIVOLCS REST is kept as supplemental official evidence.
desc_zh: 菲律宾条目在 v003 中补强为官方 PHIVOLCS 服务与 HDX 下载双路径：HDX 提供活动断层分布 KMZ，PHIVOLCS ActiveFault ArcGIS MapServer 提供国家级活动断层线服务并支持 JSON/GeoJSON 查询。这使该条既有可下载镜像，也有官方机构在线服务证据；仍需后续核对源许可和版本沿革。
desc_en: This Philippines entry is strengthened in v003 with both an official PHIVOLCS service route and an HDX download route. HDX provides the active-fault distribution KMZ, while the PHIVOLCS ActiveFault ArcGIS MapServer exposes the national active-fault polyline layer with JSON/GeoJSON query support. Source licensing and version lineage still need follow-up checks.
access_status: open humanitarian data download; source license needs verification
source_type: humanitarian data portal mirroring official geohazard data

name: NSHM23 Fault Sections Database and Earthquake Geology Inputs
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Earthquake Hazards Program
theme_raw: NSHM23 fault sections database; EQGeoDB
coverage: Western United States; related versions for U.S. NSHM 2023
license: CC0-1.0 / U.S. public domain style open data marking
formats: SHP, KML, GeoJSON, CSV tables
access: USGS data release / DOI download
doi_or_citation: Hatem et al. (2021), Earthquake geology inputs for the U.S. NSHM 2023, version 1.0, https://doi.org/10.5066/P918XCUU; later v2/v3 related releases
time_span: released 2021 with later v2/v3 updates for NSHM 2023 preparation
notes: USGS page states the data release contains a fault sections database depicting geometry of faults capable of hosting independent earthquakes and an EQGeoDB of slip-rate constraints. Formats include SHP, KML, GeoJSON and CSV.
desc_zh: NSHM23 fault sections database 是美国 2023 国家地震危险性模型的断层段输入资料，描述可独立孕震断层的几何；配套 EQGeoDB 记录断层滑动速率约束点。USGS 数据发布提供 SHP、KML、GeoJSON 和 CSV 等格式，覆盖美国西部多个州并有后续 v2/v3 版本。它比 QFFDB 更接近危险性模型输入层。
desc_en: The NSHM23 fault sections database is the fault-geometry input dataset prepared for the 2023 U.S. National Seismic Hazard Model, depicting faults capable of hosting independent earthquakes. Its companion EQGeoDB stores point slip-rate constraints. The USGS data release provides SHP, KML, GeoJSON, and CSV formats for western U.S. states, with later v2/v3 releases linked from the same record.
scale: national hazard-model fault sections; western U.S. v1/v2/v3 lineage
feature_count: ~1,000 proposed fault sections noted in Scientific Data article

name: Slab2 - A Comprehensive Subduction Zone Geometry Model
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Earthquake Hazards Program
theme_raw: subduction-zone slab geometry; megathrust interface model; slab depth/dip/strike/thickness grids
license: CC0-1.0 / U.S. public domain style open data marking
formats: NetCDF GMT grids, CSV clipping masks/supplementary nodes, KMZ, Perl query script, metadata XML
access: USGS data release; ScienceBase direct file downloads; DOI landing page
doi_or_citation: Hayes, G. (2018), Slab2 - A Comprehensive Subduction Zone Geometry Model, U.S. Geological Survey data release, https://doi.org/10.5066/F7PV6JNV
time_span: published 2018; active present-day subduction-zone geometry model
notes: Accepted as fault-source geometry rather than surface fault trace: the USGS page explicitly frames subduction interfaces as the largest earthquake-hosting faults. ScienceBase lists Slab2Distribute_Mar2018.tar.gz (~140 MB) and Slab2_depth.kmz (~1.66 MB).
desc_zh: Slab2 是 USGS 发布的全球俯冲带三维几何模型，描述活动俯冲板片/巨型逆冲界面的深度、走向、倾角、厚度和不确定性。它不是地表断层迹线库，而是用于地震和海啸危险性分析的全球板片与俯冲界面几何数据。ScienceBase 提供 NetCDF/GMT 网格、CSV 掩膜和补充节点、KMZ 与查询脚本等下载。
desc_en: Slab2 is a USGS global three-dimensional geometry model for active subduction zones, including depth, strike, dip, thickness, and uncertainty grids for slabs and megathrust-interface geometry. It is not a surface fault-trace database, but it is a key global fault-source geometry dataset for earthquake and tsunami hazard analysis. ScienceBase provides NetCDF/GMT grids, CSV masks and supplementary nodes, a KMZ, and a query script.
feature_count: global slab models; Science paper reports 24 million km2 of subducted slabs
file_size: ScienceBase lists 140.2 MB distribution tar.gz and 1.66 MB KMZ

name: PB2002 Global Plate Boundary Model
provider: Peter Bird, UCLA / Geochemistry Geophysics Geosystems publication
theme_raw: plate boundary segments; transform faults; convergent boundaries; rifts; spreading ridges; orogens
license: free academic web distribution; explicit reuse license not stated
formats: DIG text files, tables; third-party shapefile conversions exist
access: author web page; direct text-file download
doi_or_citation: Bird, P. (2003), An updated digital model of plate boundaries, Geochemistry Geophysics Geosystems, https://doi.org/10.1029/2001GC000252
time_span: present-day plate boundaries; published 2003
notes: Accepted as global plate-boundary fault/tectonic-boundary geometry. The author page states the model is distributed in four digital files; the boundary segment file is directly reachable over HTTP.
desc_zh: PB2002 是 Peter Bird 发布的全球现今板块边界数字模型，包含板块边界段、板块轮廓、造山带轮廓和板块表等文件。其边界类型覆盖大陆转换断层、洋内转换断层、俯冲/汇聚边界、裂谷和扩张脊等，适合作为全球构造边界与板块边界断层背景层。该数据较老且许可说明不如现代数据仓库清晰，使用时应引用原论文并核对再发布条件。
desc_en: PB2002 is Peter Bird’s global digital model of present-day plate boundaries, distributed as boundary segments, plate outlines, orogen outlines, and plate tables. Boundary classes include continental and oceanic transforms, convergent/subduction boundaries, rifts, and spreading ridges, making it a useful global tectonic-boundary and plate-boundary fault context layer. The dataset is older and its redistribution license is less explicit than modern repositories, so users should cite the paper and check reuse terms.
file_size: PB2002_boundaries.dig.txt about 188 kB by HTTP HEAD

name: Global Dip (G-DIP 1.0)
provider: Istituto Nazionale di Geofisica e Vulcanologia (INGV) / Seismofaults.eu
theme_raw: earthquake fault plane dip angles; CMT and literature-derived fault dips
coverage: global earthquakes near and within plate interiors, including subduction interfaces and crustal faults
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: GeoJSON ZIP, OGC Web Feature Service, documentation pages
access: Seismofaults.eu data page; WFS service; DOI citation
doi_or_citation: Basili, R. and Tiberti, M.M. (2025), Global Dip (G-DIP 1.0), INGV dataset, https://doi.org/10.13127/gdip1.0
time_span: earthquake-derived fault dip compilation; dataset published/cited 2025
notes: G-DIP contains 597 uniquely determined fault-plane dip angles for 269 earthquakes, classified by normal, reverse, transcurrent crustal, and subduction-interface reverse faulting.
desc_zh: G-DIP 1.0 是 INGV 维护的全球地震断层面倾角数据集，汇集 269 次地震对应的 597 个唯一断层面倾角判定。数据整合 Global CMT 解和文献中多种方法推断的断层面，按正断层、逆断层、走滑断层和俯冲界面逆冲事件分类。它补充了断层迹线库中常缺失的三维几何属性，可通过 GeoJSON ZIP 和 WFS 访问。
desc_en: G-DIP 1.0 is an INGV global dataset of earthquake fault-plane dip angles, compiling 597 unique dip determinations for 269 earthquakes. It combines Global CMT-derived activated nodal planes with literature-based fault-plane determinations and classifies events as normal, reverse, transcurrent crustal, or subduction-interface reverse faulting. It complements fault-trace catalogs with three-dimensional geometry attributes and is exposed through GeoJSON ZIP and WFS routes.
feature_count: 597 dip determinations; 269 earthquakes

name: SURE 2.0 Worldwide Database of Surface Ruptures
provider: University of Chieti / IRSN / ISPRA / INGV via Zenodo
theme_canonical: Global surface rupture traces and slip observations
theme_raw: surface rupturing earthquakes; rupture traces; slip measurements; fault displacement hazard
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: Shapefile ZIP, XLSX tables, TXT notes and references
access: Zenodo DOI record with direct file downloads
doi_or_citation: Nurminen et al. (2022), SURE 2.0, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.7020265; Scientific Data article https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-022-01835-z
time_span: surface-rupturing earthquakes from 1872 to 2019; version 2.0 published 2022
notes: Zenodo description states the release contains background earthquake table, slip measurement table, and joint shapefile of all mapped surface rupture traces for 50 earthquakes.
desc_zh: SURE 2.0 是全球地震地表破裂数据库，面向断层位移危险性分析整理 1872-2019 年 50 次地表破裂地震的破裂迹线和滑移观测。Zenodo 提供破裂线 Shapefile 压缩包、滑移观测表、地震背景表、说明和参考文献文件。它与活动断层长期迹线库互补，更偏向事件级破裂与位移观测。
desc_en: SURE 2.0 is a worldwide surface-rupture database for fault displacement hazard analysis, covering mapped rupture traces and slip observations for 50 surface-rupturing earthquakes from 1872 to 2019. The Zenodo record provides a rupture shapefile ZIP, slip-observation tables, earthquake background tables, notes, and references. It complements long-term active-fault trace databases with event-level rupture and displacement observations.
feature_count: 50 surface-rupturing earthquakes; rupture shapefile and slip observations
file_size: Zenodo lists 13.3 MB total; rupture ZIP about 11.0 MB

name: Database of Surface Faulting events in Italy (SFIT)
provider: Istituto Nazionale di Geofisica e Vulcanologia (INGV) / Seismofaults.eu
theme_raw: surface fault ruptures; coseismic rupture traces; georeferenced rupture observations; event images
coverage: Italy; initial release documents six major surface-faulting earthquakes
license: CC-BY-4.0 except where otherwise noted
formats: GeoJSON ZIP, OGC WMS/WFS services, web database
access: Seismofaults.eu data page and service page; DOI citation
doi_or_citation: Cinti, F.R., Pantosti, D., and Civico, R. (2026), Database of Surface Faulting events in ITaly, SFIT, https://doi.org/10.13127/sfit/sfit.1
time_span: Italian surface-faulting earthquakes over the last century; first release cited 2026
notes: SFIT documents six initial events: 1915 Avezzano, 1980 Irpinia, 1997 Umbria-Marche, 2009 L Aquila, 2016 Norcia, and 2018 Etna-Fleri; data page lists GeoJSON ZIP and OGC services.
desc_zh: SFIT 是 INGV/Seismofaults.eu 发布的意大利地表断裂事件数据库，统一整理过去一个世纪意大利地震地表破裂的点、线、照片和事件信息。首版覆盖 Avezzano、Irpinia、Umbria-Marche、L Aquila、Norcia 和 Etna-Fleri 六次主要事件，并按破裂重要性对迹线和观测进行分级。数据页提供 GeoJSON ZIP 与 OGC 服务入口。
desc_en: SFIT is an INGV/Seismofaults.eu database of surface-faulting events in Italy, harmonizing rupture traces, observation points, images, and event metadata from published sources. The first release documents six major events: Avezzano, Irpinia, Umbria-Marche, L Aquila, Norcia, and Etna-Fleri, with ranking of rupture features. The data page exposes GeoJSON ZIP and OGC service routes.
feature_count: six major events in first release

name: Utah Quaternary Faults
provider: Utah Geological Survey / Utah Geospatial Resource Center
theme_raw: Quaternary faults and folds; Utah active faults; seismic-hazard source faults
license: Utah SGID open data; attribution requested; no constraints/warranties stated
formats: ArcGIS FeatureServer, GeoJSON/PBF query, SGID/Open Data download
access: UGRC product page; ArcGIS open-data page; FeatureServer query/extract endpoint
doi_or_citation: Utah Geological Survey Quaternary Fault and Fold Database and Map of Utah; UGRC SGID Utah Quaternary Faults layer
time_span: Quaternary faults; service description says incorporated formally published sources through 2025
notes: FeatureServer layer supports Query and Extract, JSON/GeoJSON/PBF formats, and returnCountOnly reported 19,743 line features during v003 verification.
desc_zh: Utah Quaternary Faults 是犹他州地质调查局维护、UGRC/SGID 发布的第四纪活动断层与相关褶皱线数据。数据用于表征可能产生大地震的断层与褶皱，FeatureServer 支持查询和提取，并可返回 GeoJSON/PBF。v003 验证时服务计数接口返回 19,743 条线要素，说明这是可程序访问的开放服务而非静态说明页。
desc_en: Utah Quaternary Faults is the Utah Geological Survey fault-and-fold line dataset published through UGRC/SGID. It represents faults and related folds considered potential large-earthquake sources, with FeatureServer query and extract support and JSON/GeoJSON/PBF output. During v003 verification, the count endpoint returned 19,743 line features, confirming programmatic access rather than a static documentation page.
scale: state geologic hazard database; 2025 service lineage
feature_count: 19,743 line features by FeatureServer returnCountOnly

name: Quaternary faults and folds in Alaska: A digital database (MP 141)
provider: Alaska Division of Geological & Geophysical Surveys
theme_raw: Alaska Quaternary faults and folds; paleoseismic parameters; active fault traces
license: public state publication/download; use constraints to verify in metadata
formats: ESRI Shapefile, PDF report, map sheet, FGDC metadata
access: DGGS publication page with direct shapefile download and metadata
doi_or_citation: Koehler et al. (2012), Quaternary faults and folds in Alaska: A digital database, Alaska DGGS MP 141, https://doi.org/10.14509/23944
time_span: compiled 2002-2012; Quaternary faults defined as past 1.6 Ma in metadata
notes: DGGS page lists a free Shapefile download (432.1 kB), PDF report, statewide map sheet, metadata and readme. Metadata says it is a vector dataset of faults and folds believed to be sources of M>6 earthquakes.
desc_zh: Alaska DGGS MP 141 是阿拉斯加第四纪断层与褶皱数字数据库，汇总可能产生 M>6 地震的断层和相关褶皱，并记录最近变形年代、滑动速率、运动性质、倾向和制图可靠性等属性。出版页提供免费 Shapefile、报告、地图和元数据下载。它是美国 QFFDB 阿拉斯加部分的重要州级源资料。
desc_en: Alaska DGGS MP 141 is a digital database of Quaternary faults and folds in Alaska, compiling structures believed to be sources of M>6 earthquakes with attributes such as age of most recent deformation, slip rate, slip sense, dip direction, and mapping reliability. The publication page provides a free shapefile, report, map sheet, and metadata. It is an important state-level source for the Alaska portion of the U.S. Quaternary fault archive.
source_type: official state geologic survey publication/data release
scale: statewide 1:3,700,000 map sheet; source scales vary
file_size: 432.1 kB shapefile package listed by DGGS

name: Alaska Fault Trace Mapping, 2021
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Alaska Science Center
theme_raw: ArcticDEM-derived fault and fold traces; revised active faults; newly recognized active structures
coverage: Alaska Range, Brooks Range, Yukon and other Alaska regions within listed spatial extent
license: public U.S. government dataset
formats: USGS data release, XML metadata, digital geospatial data
access: Data.gov metadata; DOI landing page; USGS data release
doi_or_citation: USGS Alaska Fault Trace Mapping, 2021, DOI https://doi.org/10.5066/P9H02FXB
time_span: dataset last updated 2025-05-24 in Data.gov; based on ArcticDEM 3.0 mapping
notes: Data.gov states the dataset is a detailed 1:10,000 digital map based on ArcticDEM 3.0, revising known active faults/folds or identifying active structures not previously recognized.
desc_zh: Alaska Fault Trace Mapping, 2021 是 USGS Alaska Science Center 发布的高分辨率断层与褶皱迹线数据，基于 ArcticDEM 3.0 在约 1:10,000 尺度上识别地貌线索。数据用于修订 USGS QFFDB 中已知活动断层/褶皱的位置精度，也记录部分此前未识别的活动构造。它可作为 MP 141 与 QFFDB 之外的阿拉斯加高分辨率补充线索。
desc_en: Alaska Fault Trace Mapping, 2021 is a USGS Alaska Science Center high-resolution digital map of fault and fold traces interpreted from ArcticDEM 3.0 at about 1:10,000 scale. The dataset revises locations of known active faults and folds archived in the USGS Quaternary Fault and Fold Database and identifies some previously unrecognized active structures. It is a high-resolution Alaska supplement to MP 141 and the national QFFDB.

name: Washington Earthquakes and Faults - Quaternary Active Faults
provider: Washington Geological Survey / Washington Department of Natural Resources
theme_raw: Quaternary active faults; active folds; trenches; earthquake damage and catalogs
license: Washington DNR open data/service terms to verify
formats: ArcGIS MapServer, JSON/GeoJSON/PBF query, open data map layer
access: Washington DNR ArcGIS open-data map; ArcGIS REST MapServer layer
doi_or_citation: Washington Geological Survey Earthquakes and Faults database / Quaternary Active Faults layer
time_span: Quaternary active faults; major dataset update announced May 2025; service live in 2026
notes: ArcGIS REST service layer 12 exposes Quaternary Active Faults as polylines with JSON/GeoJSON/PBF query support; returnCountOnly reported 1,276 active-fault line features during v003 verification.
desc_zh: Washington Earthquakes and Faults 数据库包含华盛顿州地震成因要素，其中 Quaternary Active Faults 图层记录有第四纪活动证据的断层线。WADNR/Washington Geological Survey 的 REST 服务还包括活动褶皱、探槽、地震目录和历史震害等相关层。v003 验证时活动断层图层支持 JSON/GeoJSON 查询，并返回 1,276 条线要素。
desc_en: The Washington Earthquakes and Faults database contains seismogenic features for Washington State, including a Quaternary Active Faults polyline layer for faults with demonstrable Quaternary activity. The WADNR/Washington Geological Survey REST service also includes active folds, trenches, earthquake catalog layers, and historical damage. During v003 verification, the active-fault layer supported JSON/GeoJSON query output and returned 1,276 line features.
feature_count: 1,276 active-fault line features by MapServer returnCountOnly

name: Mexico Quaternary Fault Database
provider: Terra Digitalis / Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México (UNAM)
theme_raw: Mexican Quaternary faults; fault geometry; sense of movement; last displacement; slip rate; recurrence interval
license: CC-SA 4.0 stated in GeoNode restrictions; layer license field says Not Specified
formats: GeoJSON, ESRI Shapefile ZIP, GML, CSV, Excel, WMS/WFS
access: Terra Digitalis GeoNode layer; GeoServer WFS direct download; downloadable map layer
doi_or_citation: Cid Villegas, G., Ferrari, L., and Mendoza, C. (2017), Mexico Quaternary Fault Database, Terra Digitalis 1.1(9)
time_span: published in Terra Digitalis; GeoNode publication date 2022-01-21; Quaternary fault activity fields
notes: GeoNode detail page states anyone can view/download the vector layer and offers Original Dataset, Shapefile, CSV, Excel and GeoJSON downloads. A direct WFS GeoJSON request returned feature content during v004 verification.
desc_zh: Mexico Quaternary Fault Database 是 UNAM Terra Digitalis/GeoNode 发布的墨西哥第四纪断层矢量图层，汇总断层几何、运动性质、活动证据、历史地震、震级、最大位移、滑动速率、复发间隔和参考文献等字段。图层页面显示 Anyone 可下载，并提供 Shapefile、GeoJSON、CSV、Excel 和 WFS 等访问方式。该库填补了墨西哥国家级第四纪断层资料的开放入口。
desc_en: The Mexico Quaternary Fault Database is a Terra Digitalis/UNAM GeoNode vector layer synthesizing Mexican Quaternary fault geometry, movement sense, activity evidence, historical earthquakes, magnitude, maximum displacement, slip rate, recurrence interval, and references. The layer page allows public downloads and offers Shapefile, GeoJSON, CSV, Excel, and WFS access. It provides a strong open national entry point for Mexican Quaternary fault data.

name: British Columbia Digital Geology - Geology Faults
provider: Government of British Columbia / BC Geological Survey / BC Geographic Warehouse
theme_raw: bedrock geology faults; GEOL_FAULT_LINE; British Columbia Digital Geology
license: Open Government Licence - British Columbia / Canada open data portal listing
formats: SHP ZIP, KML, WMS, BC Geographic Warehouse custom download
access: Canada Open Government portal; BC Data Catalogue; direct shapefile ZIP download
doi_or_citation: Government of British Columbia, Geology Faults / British Columbia Digital Geology
time_span: 2018 shapefile package; maintained provincial digital geology layer
notes: This is a general bedrock/geologic fault layer, not an active-fault hazard database. The open portal describes it as part of British Columbia Digital Geology and lists a direct 4.7 MB shapefile ZIP plus BC Geographic Warehouse access.
desc_zh: British Columbia Geology Faults 是加拿大开放政府门户收录的省级地质断裂线数据，属于 British Columbia Digital Geology 的 bedrock geology 数据源。它不是活动断层危险性库，而是省域地质图框架中的一般断层线图层，适合用于地质构造背景、矿产/地质制图和与活动断层库的空间对照。门户提供直接 Shapefile ZIP、KML/WMS/BC Geographic Warehouse 等入口。
desc_en: British Columbia Geology Faults is a provincial geologic fault-line layer listed in Canada’s open data portal as part of British Columbia Digital Geology. It is not an active-fault hazard database; rather, it is a general bedrock-geology fault layer useful for structural context, geologic mapping, mineral studies, and comparison with active-fault datasets. The portal provides a direct shapefile ZIP plus KML/WMS/BC Geographic Warehouse access routes.
file_size: 4.7 MB shapefile ZIP by HEAD check

name: Major faults in Iran (flt2cg)
provider: U.S. Geological Survey World Energy Project
theme_raw: Iran major faults; geologic map arcs; thrust faults; petroleum province context
license: Public Domain / U.S. Government work
formats: downloadable GIS data via DOI/ScienceBase; FGDC XML metadata
access: USGS Science Data Catalog; DOI landing page; Data.gov metadata
doi_or_citation: Pollastro, R.M., Persits, F.M., and Steinshouer, D.W. (1999), Major faults in Iran (flt2cg), USGS data release, https://doi.org/10.5066/P9TMSOQ0
time_span: data dates 1999-09; USGS metadata updated 2021
notes: Accepted as a country-level major geologic fault dataset, not an active-fault inventory. The Data.gov and USGS catalog records identify access level public and geospatial downloadable GIS data.
desc_zh: Major faults in Iran (flt2cg) 是 USGS World Energy Project 发布的伊朗主要断层 GIS 数据，服务于伊朗地质图、地质省和油气资源评价背景。它不是现代活动断层数据库，但提供了国家尺度主要地质断层线索，并带有公开 DOI 和 FGDC 元数据。对伊朗活动构造研究而言，它可作为基础构造框架层，而活动性判断仍需结合 Hessami 等活动断层资料或区域研究。
desc_en: Major faults in Iran (flt2cg) is a USGS World Energy Project GIS dataset of major Iranian faults used with geologic-map and petroleum-province context. It is not a modern active-fault inventory, but it provides an open country-scale structural fault layer with DOI and FGDC metadata. For Iranian active-tectonics work, it should be treated as a background structural layer and combined with dedicated active-fault studies.
scale: country-scale geologic map / petroleum province assessment

name: SCEC Community Fault Model (CFM) v7.0
provider: Statewide California Earthquake Center (SCEC) / Zenodo
theme_raw: 3D fault surfaces; fault traces; active faults; California Community Fault Model
coverage: California and adjacent offshore basins; v7.0 spans northern Mexico to southern Cascadia
license: BSD-3-Clause
formats: GOCAD tsurf, SHP, GMT, KML, metadata spreadsheets, ZIP archive, Explorer export
access: Zenodo DOI archive; SCEC CFM Explorer; SCEC project page
doi_or_citation: Plesch, A., Marshall, S., and Shaw, J. (2024), SCEC Community Fault Model (CFM) v7.0, Zenodo, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.13685611; Plesch et al. (2007)
time_span: CFM 7.0 published 2024; statewide California active-fault geometry update
notes: Zenodo record states CFM 7.0 contains 608 fully documented fault objects across preferred, rupture, and alternative submodels; the preferred model has 556 fault objects. Archive size is 120.1 MB.
desc_zh: SCEC Community Fault Model v7.0 是加州及邻近海域活动断层的三维几何模型，整合地表迹线、地震活动、反射剖面、井资料、地质剖面和其他约束。Zenodo 归档提供 GOCAD tsurf 三维面、不同分辨率网格、Shapefile/GMT/KML 断层迹线和完整元数据；CFM Explorer 可按断层浏览和下载。v7.0 首次扩展到全加州，是二维活动断层线之外的重要三维断层几何资源。
desc_en: SCEC Community Fault Model v7.0 is a three-dimensional geometric model of active faults in California and adjacent offshore basins, constrained by surface traces, seismicity, reflection profiles, wells, geologic cross sections, and other data. The Zenodo archive provides GOCAD tsurf surfaces, multiple mesh resolutions, Shapefile/GMT/KML traces, and metadata, while the CFM Explorer supports interactive browsing and download. Version 7.0 extends coverage statewide and is a key 3D complement to 2D active-fault trace datasets.
source_type: DOI research data repository and web explorer
feature_count: 608 fully documented fault objects; 556 preferred fault objects
file_size: 120.1 MB ZIP archive listed by Zenodo

name: SCEC Geologic Slip Rate Database (GSRD)
provider: Statewide California Earthquake Center (SCEC)
theme_raw: geologic slip rate estimates; active faults; California Nevada northern Mexico
coverage: California, Nevada, small parts of Arizona and northern Mexico
license: SCEC public web database; archive DOI pending per page
formats: web explorer, CSV download for selected/all queried sites
access: SCEC database page; GSRD Explorer; CSV download through explorer selection
doi_or_citation: SCEC Geologic Slip Rate Database v2023.09; based on USGS NSHM23_EQGeoDB_v2 and UCERF3 Appendix B lineage
time_span: version 2023.09; includes studies published up to December 2020 in first release criteria
notes: Page says the full archive with DOI is coming soon, but the explorer already supports querying and downloading selected site metadata in CSV. Treat as open web database with evolving archive status, not a replacement for primary literature.
desc_zh: SCEC Geologic Slip Rate Database 汇总加州、内华达、亚利桑那局部和墨西哥北部活动断层的野外地质滑动速率估计，并将站点位置、滑动速率、不确定性和文献链接组织到可查询网页工具中。当前 v2023.09 初版主要继承 USGS NSHM23_EQGeoDB_v2 和 UCERF3 Appendix B 中符合条件的资料。Explorer 支持按断层名、站点名、坐标和速率范围查询，并下载 27 列元数据 CSV。
desc_en: The SCEC Geologic Slip Rate Database compiles field-derived geologic slip-rate estimates for active faults in California, Nevada, small parts of Arizona, and northern Mexico, linking site locations, rates, uncertainties, and source publications in a searchable web tool. The current v2023.09 release mainly carries forward qualifying records from USGS NSHM23_EQGeoDB_v2 and UCERF3 Appendix B. The Explorer supports searches by fault, site, location, and rate range, with CSV download of the full 27-column metadata for selected sites.
access_status: open web database; DOI archive pending

name: South East France Potentially Active Faults (SEFPAF)
provider: Université Grenoble Alpes / ASNR / BRGM collaborators via Zenodo
theme_raw: potentially active faults; seismogenic potential; structural components; seismic hazard source screening
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: CSV, HTML interactive map, Jupyter notebook, input CSV ZIP
access: Zenodo DOI record download; ESSD preprint documentation
doi_or_citation: Mowbray et al. (2025), South East France Potentially Active Faults, Zenodo, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.17235395; ESSD preprint https://doi.org/10.5194/essd-2025-601
time_span: dataset published 2025; ESSD discussion started 2025-12-27; potentially active fault compilation
notes: Accepted as a current Zenodo research dataset despite article/preprint discussion status. Zenodo API lists SEFPAF.csv, SEFPAF.html, compilation code, and INPUT.zip under CC BY 4.0.
desc_zh: SEFPAF 是东南法国潜在活动断层资料汇编，整合地质图、既有新构造数据库和断层专项研究中的结构、运动学和新构造信息，并给出重要性、文献完备度和潜在发震性等指标。Zenodo 提供 CSV 主表、交互式 HTML 地图、编译代码和输入 CSV 压缩包，许可为 CC BY 4.0。它适合作为低至中等地震活动区构建断层型震源模型和筛选重点断层的开放数据源。
desc_en: SEFPAF is a South-East France potentially active fault compilation integrating structural, kinematic, and neotectonic information from geologic maps, prior neotectonic databases, and fault-specific studies. It provides indices for importance, documentation level, and seismogenic potential. Zenodo distributes the main CSV, an interactive HTML map, compilation code, and input CSV archive under CC BY 4.0, making it useful for fault-based source-model screening in a low-to-moderate seismicity region.
file_size: SEFPAF.csv 2.0 MB; SEFPAF.html 21.1 MB; INPUT.zip 1.1 MB listed by Zenodo API

name: Fault database for the George and NE Edward Rifts, Uganda
provider: Zenodo; Evans (2022); University of Bristol / East African Rift collaborators
theme_raw: George and northeast Edward Rifts active fault traces
coverage: George and northeast Edward Rifts, Uganda / Albertine Rift, East African Rift System
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: ESRI Shapefile ZIP, GeoJSON, KMZ, Excel slip-profile summary, slip-profile data ZIP
access: Zenodo DOI repository with direct file downloads
doi_or_citation: Evans (2022), Fault database for the George and NE Edward Rifts, Uganda, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.6510002
time_span: Zenodo publication 2022; mapped active faults and slip-profile measurements for George and NE Edward Rift study area
notes: Open CC-BY Zenodo data package. Files include EGR_faults.shp.zip, EGR_faults.geojson, EGR_faults.kmz, EGR_slip_profiles_summary.xlsx and slip_profile_data.zip. This is narrower and more openly downloadable than the restricted 2025 northern Western Branch EARS Zenodo record, which is deferred in v005.
desc_zh: 该数据集是乌干达 George Rift 与东北 Edward Rift 的活动断层 GIS 数据包，覆盖东非裂谷 Albertine Rift 的局部区域。Zenodo 记录以 CC-BY-4.0 发布，提供 Shapefile、GeoJSON、KMZ、滑动剖面汇总表和剖面数据压缩包，适合与 Malawi、Luangwa 等东非裂谷活动断层数据做区域对比。
desc_en: This dataset provides active-fault GIS traces for the George and northeast Edward Rifts in Uganda, within the Albertine sector of the East African Rift System. The Zenodo record is released under CC-BY-4.0 and includes Shapefile, GeoJSON, KMZ, slip-profile summary, and slip-profile data files. It is useful for comparing localized Albertine Rift fault mapping with the Malawi and Luangwa active-fault datasets.
feature_count: Feature count not stated in API; file package includes vector traces and slip-profile observations
file_size: EGR_faults.geojson 179.0 kB; EGR_faults.shp.zip 56.5 kB; KMZ 65.9 kB; slip_profile_data.zip 499.9 kB listed by Zenodo API

name: New Zealand Community Fault Model (NZ CFM) v1.0
provider: GNS Science / Earth Sciences New Zealand and national collaborators
theme_raw: New Zealand two- and three-dimensional active and potentially active fault-zone model
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: XLSX parameter table, Shapefile ZIP, GOCAD T-surf ZIP, GIS projects ZIP, tectonic-domain Shapefile, CSV attributes ZIP, PDF report, Geo3D web viewer
access: GNS data-and-resources page with direct downloads and Geo3D viewer
doi_or_citation: Seebeck et al. (2022), New Zealand Community Fault Model - version 1.0, GNS Science Report 2021/57, doi:10.21420/GA7S-BS61
time_span: Version 1.0 released 2022; first 2D/3D community model for active and potentially active New Zealand fault zones
notes: GNS page states the model is a 2D and 3D representation of active and potentially active fault zones and is open, updatable and publicly available. Downloads include shapefile, GOCAD surfaces, parameters, GIS projects and report; page reports nearly 900 fault zones.
desc_zh: NZ CFM v1.0 是 GNS Science 主导的社区断层模型，表达新西兰板块边界沿线活动和潜在活动断层带的二维迹线与三维断层面。数据页提供 Shapefile、GOCAD T-surf、参数表、GIS 项目、CSV 属性和报告，并以 CC-BY-4.0 授权，适合地震源建模、地震动模拟和三维断层几何研究。
desc_en: NZ CFM v1.0 is a GNS-led community fault model representing active and potentially active fault zones along the New Zealand plate boundary in both 2D and 3D. The data page provides Shapefile, GOCAD T-surf, parameter tables, GIS projects, CSV attributes, and documentation under CC-BY-4.0. It is appropriate for earthquake-source modelling, ground-motion simulation, and 3D fault-geometry work.
scale: national 2D/3D active-fault model; nominal compilation scale 1:500,000-1:1,000,000 per report
feature_count: Nearly 900 fault zones reported by GNS overview
file_size: Shapefile ZIP 171.6 kB; GNS report PDF 10.0 MB; other download sizes listed on GNS page

name: New Zealand Paleoseismic Site Database v1.0
provider: GNS Science / Earth Sciences New Zealand
theme_canonical: Paleoseismic sites and fault slip observations
theme_raw: New Zealand paleoseismic slip-rate, earthquake-timing and single-event-displacement site database
license: GNS terms / likely institutional open download; page does not state CC license explicitly
formats: XLSX spreadsheet, PDF design report, PDF data dictionary
access: GNS data-and-resources page with direct XLSX and documentation downloads
doi_or_citation: Litchfield et al. (2022), New Zealand Paleoseismic Site Database: design and overview of version 1.0, GNS Science Report 2021/52, doi:10.21420/VTPT-KB52
time_span: Version 1.0 compiled from 2020-2021 work and released 2022 for NSHM 2022
notes: The overview page states the database groups paleoseismic data into Slip Rate, Earthquake Timings, and Single-Event Displacement worksheets, with Easting/Northing site attributes. The report states 2,136 sites, 68 combined records, 862 slip-rate sites, 304 earthquake-timing sites and 970 single-event-displacement sites.
desc_zh: 新西兰古地震站点数据库 v1.0 汇总活动断层上的古地震观测站点，分为滑动速率、地震时序/复发间隔和单次事件位移三类工作表。GNS 页面提供 XLSX 数据和设计报告/数据字典，报告记录 2,136 个站点及相关组合记录，可用于约束新西兰活动断层模型和国家地震危险性模型。
desc_en: The New Zealand Paleoseismic Site Database v1.0 compiles site observations along active faults into slip-rate, earthquake-timing/recurrence, and single-event-displacement worksheets. GNS provides the XLSX database plus design and data-dictionary reports. The overview report documents 2,136 sites and associated combined records, making it a key observational companion to New Zealand active-fault and seismic-hazard models.
access_status: open official spreadsheet download; terms should be checked before redistribution
scale: national paleoseismic site database linked to active faults
feature_count: 2,136 sites and 68 combined records reported in GNS report; worksheets include 862 slip-rate sites, 304 earthquake-timing sites and 970 SED sites
file_size: XLSX 798.8 kB; overview report PDF 6.2 MB; data dictionary PDF 1.2 MB from HEAD checks

name: Idaho Geological Survey Active Fault Database v202505
provider: Idaho Geological Survey / University of Idaho ArcGIS Online
theme_raw: Idaho active or potentially active fault traces
license: ArcGIS item license not specified; public feature service
formats: ArcGIS FeatureServer, JSON, GeoJSON, PBF
access: Public ArcGIS FeatureServer with query and GeoJSON output
doi_or_citation: Idaho Geological Survey Active Fault Database v202505 ArcGIS item and FeatureServer
time_span: v202505 service; includes Quaternary, Holocene and other active/potentially active fault attributes where available
notes: ArcGIS item snippet says the layer depicts active or potentially active faults mapped in Idaho. The real FeatureServer layer id is 116, not 0. Query returned 4,290 line features and attributes including fault name, sense of motion, dip direction, slip rate, age, synopsis and USGS fault number.
desc_zh: Idaho Geological Survey Active Fault Database v202505 是爱达荷州活动或潜在活动断层线要素服务。ArcGIS FeatureServer 可直接查询 JSON/GeoJSON，当前 layer 116 返回 4,290 条线要素，并包含断层名、运动性质、倾向、滑动速率、年龄、摘要和 USGS 断层编号等属性。
desc_en: The Idaho Geological Survey Active Fault Database v202505 is a public line-feature service for active or potentially active faults in Idaho. The ArcGIS FeatureServer supports JSON and GeoJSON queries; layer 116 currently returns 4,290 line features with fault name, sense of motion, dip direction, slip rate, age, synopsis, and USGS fault-number attributes.
access_status: open public feature service; license field empty
feature_count: 4,290 line features returned by FeatureServer count query on layer 116
file_size: service query, no static package size

name: Oregon Fault Lines
provider: Oregon Department of Geology and Mineral Industries (DOGAMI) / Oregon GEOHub
theme_raw: Oregon statewide structural fault lines from geologic data compilation
license: License not specified on ArcGIS item; public Oregon GEOHub service
formats: ArcGIS MapServer feature layer, JSON, GeoJSON
