access: Oregon GEOHub item and public MapServer layer query
doi_or_citation: DOGAMI / Oregon Geologic Data Compilation - 2009; Oregon GEOHub Fault Lines item
time_span: Oregon Geologic Data Compilation - 2009; ArcGIS item modified 2024
notes: Accepted as a general geologic/structural fault layer, not an active-fault inventory. The MapServer layer description says it represents structural faults at original mapping scale and descriptive data. Query returned 52,957 line features with fault type, certainty, planar direction/degree and age fields. Oregon active-fault service found in data.gov was deferred because its DOGAMI StatewideEQ MapServer was not started during v005 checks.
desc_zh: Oregon Fault Lines 是 DOGAMI/Oregon GEOHub 发布的全州结构断层线数据，源自 Oregon Geologic Data Compilation 2009。该图层不是专门的活动断层库，但可作为俄勒冈地质断层背景数据使用；MapServer 查询返回 52,957 条线要素，并含断层类型、确定性、断层面方向和年龄等字段。
desc_en: Oregon Fault Lines is a statewide DOGAMI/Oregon GEOHub structural-fault layer derived from the Oregon Geologic Data Compilation 2009. It is not an active-fault inventory, but it is useful as general geologic fault context for Oregon. The MapServer query returned 52,957 line features with fields for fault type, certainty, planar direction, planar degree, and age.
feature_count: 52,957 line features returned by MapServer count query
file_size: service query, no static package size

name: Great Basin Quaternary Fault Slip and Dilation Tendency Shapefile
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Geology, Minerals, Energy, and Geophysics Science Center
theme_raw: Slip tendency and dilation tendency calculated for Quaternary faults in the Great Basin
coverage: Great Basin region, including parts of Arizona, California, Idaho, Nevada, Oregon, Utah and Wyoming
license: CC0 1.0 Universal
formats: Shapefile ZIP, KMZ ZIP, FGDC/XML metadata via ScienceBase
access: USGS data release and ScienceBase file downloads
doi_or_citation: Siler (2022), Shapefile for slip tendency and dilation tendency calculated for Quaternary faults in the Great Basin, https://doi.org/10.5066/P9YL58W6
time_span: USGS data release 2022; Quaternary faults evaluated under ambient stress conditions
notes: This is a derived attribute dataset, not an independent active-fault map. It adds slip-tendency and dilation-tendency values to Quaternary fault surfaces in the Great Basin and includes full-study and INGENIOUS-area shapefile/KMZ packages. USGS page marks the work CC0.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 数据发布为 Great Basin 区域的第四纪断层计算滑动倾向和张裂倾向，覆盖亚利桑那、加州、爱达荷、内华达、俄勒冈、犹他和怀俄明的部分地区。它不是新的断层制图，而是在第四纪断层基础上增加应力取向相关属性，提供 Shapefile 与 KMZ 下载，适合地热、构造应力和活动断层筛选分析。
desc_en: This USGS data release calculates slip tendency and dilation tendency for Quaternary faults in the Great Basin, including parts of Arizona, California, Idaho, Nevada, Oregon, Utah, and Wyoming. It is a derived attribute dataset rather than a new fault map, adding stress-orientation metrics to fault surfaces. Shapefile and KMZ packages make it useful for geothermal, tectonic-stress, and active-fault screening analyses.
feature_count: Not stated in USGS summary; package covers full study area and INGENIOUS area subsets
file_size: ScienceBase lists Shapefile_Full Study.zip 35.9 MB, Shapefile_INGENIOUS area.zip 28.7 MB, KMZ packages 5.6-6.9 MB

name: California Alquist-Priolo Earthquake Fault Zones
provider: California Geological Survey / California Department of Conservation
theme_raw: Alquist-Priolo Earthquake Fault Zones and associated official fault traces
license: California Department of Conservation disclaimer / public ArcGIS service
formats: ArcGIS FeatureServer, JSON, GeoJSON, PBF, metadata XML
access: ArcGIS Online item and public FeatureServer layer
doi_or_citation: California Geological Survey, Seismic Hazards Program, Alquist-Priolo Earthquake Fault Zones ArcGIS item
time_span: ArcGIS item publication date 2018-01-11 in metadata; regulatory zones reflect official Alquist-Priolo maps
notes: Accepted as a regulatory surface-rupture hazard zone dataset distinct from the broader California Fault Activity Map already cataloged. The item metadata states the polygons delineate areas where surface fault rupture previously occurred or where conditions indicate potential permanent ground displacement requiring avoidance mitigation. FeatureServer layer id is 1 and count query returned 133 polygon features.
desc_zh: California Alquist-Priolo Earthquake Fault Zones 是加州地质调查局发布的法定地表破裂危险区数据，表达官方 Alquist-Priolo 断层带边界。它不同于普通活动断层线图，重点是监管用途的多边形分区；FeatureServer layer 1 可查询 GeoJSON/PBF，当前返回 133 个多边形要素。
desc_en: California Alquist-Priolo Earthquake Fault Zones is a California Geological Survey regulatory surface-rupture hazard dataset representing official Alquist-Priolo zone boundaries. It differs from a general active-fault line map by emphasizing legally defined polygon zones for fault-rupture avoidance. FeatureServer layer 1 supports GeoJSON/PBF queries and currently returns 133 polygon features.
access_status: open public feature service; state disclaimer applies
scale: state regulatory surface-fault rupture zone polygons
feature_count: 133 polygon features returned by FeatureServer count query on layer 1
file_size: service query, no static package size

name: Fault2SHA Central Apennines Database
provider: Institute for Risk and Disaster Reduction, University College London; PANGAEA; Fault2SHA authors
theme_raw: Central Apennines active fault trace, slip-rate, slip-vector and geometry data for seismic hazard assessment
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: GeoPackage, KML, KMZ, XLSX/XLSB spreadsheets, TXT/map-coordinate ZIPs
access: PANGAEA DOI landing page and direct file downloads
doi_or_citation: Faure Walker et al. (2020), Fault2SHA Central Apennines Database, https://doi.org/10.1594/PANGAEA.922582; Scientific Data 2021 https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-021-00868-0
time_span: Published 2020; companion Scientific Data paper 2021; Central Apennines active fault observations compiled from field and literature data
notes: Accepted as a high-resolution regional active-fault field database distinct from DISS/ITHACA. PANGAEA abstract states it includes trace, fault and master-fault locations with activity and location certainties, plus slip-rate, slip-vector and surface-geometry data. Textfile metadata lists 10 downloadable data objects including GeoPackage, KML/KMZ and spreadsheets.
desc_zh: Fault2SHA Central Apennines Database 是意大利中亚平宁地区面向地震危险性分析的高分辨率活动断层观测数据库。PANGAEA 记录提供 GeoPackage、KML/KMZ、XLSX/XLSB 表格和坐标文件，包含断层迹线、断层/主断层位置、活动性与位置可信度、滑动速率、滑动矢量和地表几何信息，可作为 DISS/ITHACA 之外的更细粒度野外数据源。
desc_en: The Fault2SHA Central Apennines Database is a high-resolution active-fault field database for seismic-hazard analysis in the central Apennines of Italy. PANGAEA provides GeoPackage, KML/KMZ, XLSX/XLSB tables, and coordinate files with fault traces, fault and master-fault locations, activity and location certainty, slip-rate, slip-vector, and surface-geometry observations. It complements lower-resolution source models such as DISS and national databases such as ITHACA.
feature_count: PANGAEA lists 10 downloadable data objects; feature count varies by GeoPackage/spreadsheet layer
file_size: GeoPackage 1.4 MB; KML 2.5 MB; KMZ 456.4 kB; XLSX 1.1 MB; coordinate ZIPs 174.6-214.6 kB per PANGAEA text metadata

name: Slovenia Seismogenic Fault and Area Sources for SHMS21
provider: Slovenian Environment Agency (ARSO); Geological Survey of Slovenia (GeoZS); PANGAEA
theme_raw: Seismogenic fault and area sources for the 2021 Slovenian probabilistic seismic hazard model
license: CC-BY-SA-4.0
formats: Shapefile ZIPs, Excel parameter tables, PANGAEA ZIP package
access: PANGAEA DOI landing page and direct ZIP download
doi_or_citation: Atanackov et al. (2022), Seismogenic fault and area sources for probabilistic seismic hazard model in Slovenia, https://doi.org/10.1594/PANGAEA.940100
time_span: Published 2022 for the 2021 Slovenian PSHA model (SHMS21)
notes: PANGAEA abstract states the database contains 89 seismogenic faults and 18 area sources in Slovenia and surroundings. Data include shapefiles for seismogenic-source geometry and Excel parameter tables linked through source ID and name. This row represents the SHMS21 source model, while Database of Active Faults in Slovenia remains a related literature lead.
desc_zh: Slovenia SHMS21 地震源数据集由 ARSO 与 GeoZS 为 2021 年斯洛文尼亚概率地震危险性模型建立，PANGAEA 以 CC-BY-SA-4.0 发布。数据包含 89 个发震断层源和 18 个面源，提供描述发震源几何的 Shapefile 与按源 ID/名称关联的 Excel 参数表，适合国家地震危险性模型和跨境地震源对比。
desc_en: The Slovenia SHMS21 seismogenic-source dataset was developed by ARSO and GeoZS for the 2021 Slovenian probabilistic seismic hazard model and is distributed through PANGAEA under CC-BY-SA-4.0. It contains 89 seismogenic faults and 18 area sources, with shapefiles for source geometry and Excel parameter tables linked by source ID and name. It is useful for national seismic-hazard modelling and cross-border source-model comparison.
scale: national seismogenic fault and area-source model
feature_count: 89 seismogenic faults and 18 area sources reported by PANGAEA
file_size: 287.4 kB PANGAEA dataset; ZIP HEAD returned 294,343 bytes

name: International Database of Glacially Induced Faults
provider: PANGAEA; Geological surveys and glacially induced faulting authors
theme_raw: Confirmed and proposed glacially induced / post-glacial fault GIS inventory
coverage: Northern Fennoscandia, northwestern Russia, and proposed examples in formerly glaciated regions including southern Sweden, Denmark, Germany, Iceland, Canada and Antarctica
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: ZIP package with GIS files; PANGAEA metadata
access: PANGAEA DOI landing page and direct ZIP download
doi_or_citation: Munier et al. (2020), International database of Glacially Induced Faults, https://doi.org/10.1594/PANGAEA.922705
time_span: Published 2020; database intended for continual updates as new results emerge
notes: Accepted as a specialized fault class rather than ordinary tectonic active faults. PANGAEA abstract states the GIS inventory includes confirmed and proposed glacially induced faults, with confirmed GIFs in northern Fennoscandia including northwestern Russia and proposed GIFs elsewhere. Direct ZIP file is stable as Glacially-Induced_Faults.zip.
desc_zh: International Database of Glacially Induced Faults 汇集冰川加载/卸载诱发或再活化断层的 GIS 库，重点包括北部芬诺斯堪的亚和俄罗斯西北部确认的 GIF，也列入南瑞典、丹麦、德国等地区的拟议案例。它是专门的冰后/冰川诱发断层目录，不能与普通活动构造断层直接等同，但对古地震、冰川均衡调整和稳定大陆区地震危险性研究很有价值。
desc_en: The International Database of Glacially Induced Faults is a GIS inventory of faults induced or reactivated by glacial loading and unloading. It emphasizes confirmed GIFs in northern Fennoscandia and northwestern Russia and includes proposed cases in southern Sweden, Denmark, Germany and other formerly glaciated regions. It is a specialized post-glacial fault catalogue rather than a general active-tectonic fault database, but is valuable for paleoseismology, glacial isostatic adjustment, and stable-continental-region hazard studies.
feature_count: PANGAEA size reports one ZIP data object; feature count should be checked from GIS contents
file_size: PANGAEA dataset 161.1 kB; direct ZIP HEAD returned 164,948 bytes

name: Nevada Quaternary Faults Map Service
provider: Nevada Bureau of Mines and Geology (NBMG) / University of Nevada, Reno
theme_raw: Nevada Quaternary faults and historical ruptures by age
license: NBMG public service disclaimer; explicit reuse license not stated
formats: ArcGIS MapServer, JSON, GeoJSON, PBF
access: NBMG public MapServer and query endpoints
doi_or_citation: Nevada Bureau of Mines and Geology, Quaternary Faults in Nevada / M167-derived public map service
time_span: Service reflects Quaternary faults adapted from NBMG M167 and USGS QFFD; service checked 2026-07-09
notes: Accepted as a state service/derivative view with explicit overlap to USGS QFFD. Service description says the Quaternary faults were adapted and modified from NBMG M167 and the USGS Quaternary Fault and Fold Database, with original traces digitized at 1:250,000 scale. Layers include Historical Ruptures and Quaternary Faults by Age; layer 1 count query returned 30,652 line features.
desc_zh: Nevada Quaternary Faults Map Service 是 NBMG/UNR 发布的内华达第四纪断层与历史破裂 ArcGIS 服务。服务说明明确其数据改编自 NBMG M167 和 USGS Quaternary Fault and Fold Database，原始迹线按 1:250,000 比例尺数字化；layer 1 可查询 GeoJSON/PBF，返回 30,652 条按最后破裂年龄分类的线要素。该条目应作为州级派生服务使用，而不是独立替代 USGS QFFD。
desc_en: The Nevada Quaternary Faults Map Service is an NBMG/UNR ArcGIS service for Nevada Quaternary faults and historical ruptures. The service description states that the faults were adapted and modified from NBMG M167 and the USGS Quaternary Fault and Fold Database, with original traces digitized at 1:250,000 scale. Layer 1 supports GeoJSON/PBF queries and returned 30,652 line features symbolized by age of last rupture. It should be treated as a state derivative service, not a replacement for the USGS QFFD backbone.
access_status: open public map service; license unspecified
feature_count: Layer 1 Quaternary Faults by Age count query returned 30,652 line features; layer 0 Historical Ruptures also returned 30,652 in v006 check
file_size: service query, no static package size

name: Active Faults of El Salvador
provider: José Martínez-Díaz and collaborators via Figshare
theme_raw: El Salvador active fault traces, major faults and fault table
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: Shapefile tar.gz packages, CSV table tar.gz, Figshare metadata/API
access: Figshare DOI record and direct ndownloader file links
doi_or_citation: Martínez-Díaz et al. Figshare dataset, https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.13160165; related paper https://doi.org/10.1016/j.jsames.2020.103038
time_span: Posted 2020; linked to 2020 Journal of South American Earth Sciences active-fault study
notes: Figshare description states the data set contains three compressed files: Fault Traces with all active faults identified in El Salvador from geomorphological and field work, Major Faults as shapefile traces, and Table Faults as CSV attributes. Accepted at national detail despite existing broader GEM Central America and Caribbean row.
desc_zh: Active Faults of El Salvador 是萨尔瓦多活动断层开放数据包，Figshare 以 CC-BY-4.0 发布。数据包括所有由地貌和野外工作识别的活动断层迹线、解释的主要断层 Shapefile，以及包含断层段/主断层属性的 CSV 表，可补充已有中美洲-加勒比区域级活动断层行。
desc_en: Active Faults of El Salvador is an open Figshare dataset released under CC-BY-4.0. It includes active-fault traces identified from geomorphological and field work, interpreted major-fault shapefiles, and a CSV table linking fault sections to major faults and attributes. It adds national-scale detail beyond the broader GEM Central America and Caribbean active-fault compilation.
scale: national active-fault GIS traces and attributes
feature_count: Feature count not stated in Figshare API; files include Fault_traces, Major_Faults and Table_Faults packages
file_size: Fault_traces.tar.gz 148.6 kB; Major_Faults.tar.gz 19.0 kB; Table_Faults.csv.tar.gz 2.1 kB from Figshare API

name: Suez Rift active tectonics fault-map repository
provider: Fernández-Blanco, de Gelder and Jackson via Figshare
theme_raw: Suez Rift georeferenced fault maps including newly mapped active faults
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: RAR archives with georeferenced shapefiles and supporting geomorphic-analysis data
access: Figshare DOI record and direct ndownloader file links
doi_or_citation: Fernández-Blanco, de Gelder and Jackson (2025), Figshare dataset, https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.29176643
time_span: Posted 2025; supports geomorphic analysis of active tectonics in the Suez Rift
notes: Accepted as a regional research dataset rather than a national active-fault database. Figshare description says DR3 contains fault maps including newly mapped active faults as georeferenced shapefile; other archives contain rift units, geological units and river/drainage analysis. DR3 direct file size is 614.9 kB.
desc_zh: 该 Figshare 仓储支撑 “Is the Suez Rift in its post-rift phase?” 研究，包含苏伊士裂谷活动构造地貌分析相关的地理配准数据。其 DR3 文件明确为包含新制图活动断层的断层图 Shapefile，适合作为埃及苏伊士裂谷区域活动断层/活动构造制图线索，但不应等同于国家级完整活动断层库。
desc_en: This Figshare repository supports the study “Is the Suez Rift in its post-rift phase?” and provides georeferenced data for active-tectonics geomorphic analysis of the Suez Rift. Its DR3 archive is described as fault maps including newly mapped active faults in georeferenced shapefile format. It is best treated as a regional active-fault/active-tectonics mapping dataset, not as a complete national active-fault inventory.
feature_count: Feature count not stated; DR3 archive contains georeferenced fault maps including newly mapped active faults
file_size: DR3.rar 614.9 kB; full Figshare download all 40.93 MB listed by page

name: Mainland China Surface Rupture Database (MCSRD) v1.0
provider: China Earthquake Disaster Prevention Centre and collaborators via Zenodo / Frontiers
theme_raw: Mainland China historical and instrumental earthquake surface rupture polylines and event points
license: CC-BY-4.0
formats: RAR archive via Zenodo; spatial line and point shapefile structure described in Frontiers article
access: Zenodo DOI repository; Frontiers data-report article and Zenodo API metadata
doi_or_citation: Zhang et al. (2026), The mainland China surface rupture database (MCSRD), Frontiers in Earth Science, https://doi.org/10.3389/feart.2026.1838280; Zenodo https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.19198199
time_span: Zenodo publication 2026-03-24; database covers 72 historical and instrumental mainland-China surface-rupturing earthquakes up to 2025 according to article
notes: Accepted as a surface-rupture database, not a general active-fault map. Zenodo metadata describes a unified open-access spatial database digitized from over 600 articles and reports, with detailed rupture polylines, rupture length, maximum horizontal/vertical displacement and kinematic types. The Zenodo file is named Sample_Data.rar (125.0 kB), so downstream users should inspect repository contents before assuming a full production export.
desc_zh: MCSRD v1.0 是中国大陆地震地表破裂数据库，Zenodo 以 CC-BY-4.0 发布，并有 2026 年 Frontiers 数据报告说明。文章描述其覆盖 72 次中国大陆历史和仪器记录地表破裂地震，包含震源地震点 Shapefile 与地表破裂线 Shapefile 结构，记录破裂长度、最大垂直/水平位移、运动类型和可靠性等级；它应作为地表破裂数据库，而不是普通活动断层底图使用。
desc_en: MCSRD v1.0 is a Mainland China earthquake surface-rupture database released on Zenodo under CC-BY-4.0 and documented by a 2026 Frontiers data report. The article describes coverage of 72 historical and instrumental surface-rupturing earthquakes in mainland China, with source-earthquake point shapefiles and surface-rupture polyline shapefiles recording rupture length, maximum vertical and horizontal displacement, kinematic type, and reliability class. It should be treated as a surface-rupture database rather than a general active-fault basemap.
access_status: open Zenodo repository; file naming/content requires downstream inspection
source_type: DOI research repository record and data-report article
feature_count: 72 historical and instrumental earthquakes reported by article; polyline feature count not stated in metadata
file_size: Sample_Data.rar 125.0 kB listed by Zenodo API

license: CC-BY-4.0

time_span: Repository updated 2026; CRESCENT CFM v1.0 under review according to project page

README states all data files are vector GIS files in crescent_cfm_files and lists crustal traces, 3D crustal faults, CASIE21, Slab2, McCrory and other Cascadia interface meshes. Crustal traces GeoJSON contains 329 features; 3D 5 km GeoJSON contains 150 features in the checked files.
desc_zh: CRESCENT Cascadia CFM 仓库公开了 Cascadia 区域社区断层模型的 GIS 文件和构建脚本，许可为 CC-BY-4.0。数据包括地壳断层迹线、三维地壳断层面、Cascadia 板块边界/俯冲界面网格以及若干输入断层数据；项目页说明 v1.0 仍在 review，因此应作为开放仓储/预发布模型使用，而不是最终正式版本。
desc_en: The CRESCENT Cascadia CFM repository provides open GIS files and build scripts for a Cascadia community fault model under CC-BY-4.0. It includes crustal fault traces, 3D crustal fault surfaces, Cascadia plate-boundary and subduction-interface meshes, and supporting input datasets. Because the project page states that v1.0 is under review, the catalog treats it as an open repository/pre-release model rather than a finalized reviewed release.
access_status: open GitHub repository; model version under review
feature_count: 329 crustal-trace features in checked GeoJSON; 150 features in 3D 5 km GeoJSON sample; additional interface meshes included
file_size: crustal traces GeoJSON 954.4 kB; crustal 3D GeoPackage 13.5 MB; full 3D GeoJSON 81.5 MB from GitHub API

Item description says the dataset contains linear structural elements including thrust faults, normal faults, transverse faults and fold axes in the Rocky Mountains and Foothills, and unspecified faults in the Canadian Shield/Athabasca Basin. FeatureServer count query returned 943 line features; field sample includes Type=Fault.
desc_zh: Alberta Structures 是 Alberta Geological Survey 的基岩地质结构线数据，包含落基山和山麓带的逆冲断层、正断层、横向断层以及褶皱轴，并在加拿大地盾和 Athabasca Basin 中表达未细分类型的断层。它不是活动断层库，但作为省级开放地质断裂/构造线服务很有价值；FeatureServer 可查询 GeoJSON/PBF，当前返回 943 条线要素。
desc_en: The Alberta Structures dataset is an Alberta Geological Survey bedrock-geology structural line layer containing thrust, normal, and transverse faults plus fold axes in the Rocky Mountains and Foothills, and unspecified faults in the Canadian Shield and Athabasca Basin. It is not an active-fault database, but it is a useful provincial open geologic-fault/structure service. The FeatureServer supports GeoJSON/PBF queries and returned 943 line features.
access_status: open public service under Alberta open government licence
feature_count: 943 line features returned by FeatureServer count query
file_size: service query, no static package size

notes: Statewide 1:100,000 bedrock geology line layer focused on faults and contacts. Hub item gives CC BY-SA and cites USGS SIM 3184; Hub download API returned shapefile and GeoJSON successfully, while the underlying ANR MapServer was too slow for final verified URL evidence.
desc_zh: Vermont 断层与接触线数据来自 1:100,000 Vermont 基岩地质图的线要素层，由 Vermont 开放地理门户发布，并引用 USGS Scientific Investigations Map 3184 作为源图。ArcGIS Hub item 标示 CC BY-SA，可通过 Hub 下载 API 获取 Shapefile、GeoJSON 等格式，适合补充美国东北部基岩构造与断层线资料。
desc_en: This Vermont faults-and-contacts layer is the line component of the 1:100,000 Bedrock Geologic Map of Vermont, published through the Vermont open geodata portal and citing USGS Scientific Investigations Map 3184. The ArcGIS Hub item states CC BY-SA and exposes direct Hub downloads such as Shapefile and GeoJSON, making it a useful state-scale source for bedrock structures and faults in the northeastern United States.
access_status: open public ArcGIS Hub download and service
source_type: official state open-data portal / ArcGIS Hub feature layer
feature_count: not counted in this pass; Hub layer contains statewide faults and contacts
file_size: dynamic ArcGIS Hub downloads; content length not fixed

name: Connecticut Bedrock Geology Contacts and Faults
provider: Connecticut Department of Energy and Environmental Protection (CTDEEP) / CT Geodata Portal
theme_raw: Connecticut Bedrock Geology Contacts layer, fault-coded features
license: CC0 per ArcGIS item licenseInfo
formats: ArcGIS FeatureServer, JSON/GeoJSON query, CSV, Shapefile, GeoPackage, KML and related Hub exports
access: Public ArcGIS feature service with line layer and query endpoint; Hub export generation was observed as asynchronous for bulk downloads
doi_or_citation: CTDEEP Bedrock Geology Set, CT Geodata Portal ArcGIS item a765e96b0c05413da1dcbea0ae86707d
time_span: Static compiled 1:50,000 bedrock geology; ArcGIS item created 2019 and service checked 2026-07-13
notes: Line layer includes contacts, faults and terrane boundaries; fault-like records are identifiable through AV_LEGEND=Fault and definitions such as Fault, High angle fault and Thrust fault. Query count returned 5,518 total line features and 2,407 fault-coded line features.
desc_zh: Connecticut Bedrock Geology Contacts 是 CTDEEP 发布的 1:50,000 基岩地质线要素服务，包含接触线、断层和地体边界。服务字段中 `AV_LEGEND=Fault` 可筛出断层类要素，本轮查询到 2,407 条 fault-coded 线要素；ArcGIS item 标示 CC0，适合作为美国东北部州级基岩断层数据源。
desc_en: The Connecticut Bedrock Geology Contacts layer is a 1:50,000 state bedrock-geology line service from CTDEEP containing contacts, faults, and terrane boundaries. Fault-coded features can be selected with `AV_LEGEND=Fault`; this pass counted 2,407 such line features out of 5,518 total line records. The ArcGIS item lists CC0, making it a strong open state-scale source for bedrock fault and contact geometry.
access_status: open public ArcGIS service; bulk Hub exports may be generated asynchronously
source_type: official state open-data portal / ArcGIS feature service
feature_count: 2,407 fault-coded line features; 5,518 total contact/fault/terrane-boundary line features counted from ArcGIS query
file_size: dynamic ArcGIS service; bulk Hub exports generated on request

notes: Regional polyline layer exposes fault/dyke codes and descriptions through a public FeatureServer. This pass counted 20,622 features. The portal item is public but does not state a machine-readable reuse license, so redistribution terms should be checked before republishing derived files.
desc_zh: SADC 2008 Geology Faults and Dykes 是南非 Council for Geoscience 门户发布的南部非洲区域线要素服务，图层名为 `SADC2008_Faults&Dykes`。服务通过 ArcGIS FeatureServer 提供 JSON/GeoJSON/PBF 查询，本轮计数 20,622 条线要素，适合补足南部非洲地质断层/岩墙构造线索；但门户 item 未写明明确开放许可，二次分发前需复核条款。
desc_en: SADC 2008 Geology Faults and Dykes is a regional southern-African polyline service published through the Council for Geoscience ArcGIS portal. The `SADC2008_Faults&Dykes` layer supports JSON, GeoJSON, and PBF queries and returned 20,622 features in this pass. It is valuable for southern-African geological fault and dyke structures, although the public portal item does not state a clear reuse license.
access_status: open public feature service; reuse license not stated on item
source_type: official geological survey ArcGIS Enterprise feature service
feature_count: 20,622 line features counted from ArcGIS query
file_size: dynamic ArcGIS service

name: Quaternary faults offshore of California
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Pacific Coastal and Marine Science Center
theme_canonical: Offshore Quaternary fault traces and attributes
theme_raw: Quaternary faults offshore of California
coverage: California State and Federal offshore waters, Pacific Ocean, United States
license: U.S. public domain / Public Domain Mark 1.0
formats: Shapefile polyline zip, KML, FGDC XML metadata, PDF source-reference list, ScienceBase WMS/WFS
access: USGS Science Data Catalog and ScienceBase data release with downloadable shapefile and KML package
doi_or_citation: Walton, M.A.L., Papesh, A.G., Johnson, S.Y., Conrad, J.E., and Brothers, D.S. (2020), Quaternary faults offshore of California, U.S. Geological Survey data release, https://doi.org/10.5066/P91RYEZ4
time_span: Source mapping/data 1982-2020; publication 2020-04-30
notes: Comprehensive offshore California Quaternary-fault compilation with mapped geometries, attributes, ages, slip-rate fields and source references. This v013 pass treats it as the canonical statewide offshore fault layer and defers adding individual DS 781 California State Waters map-area fault files as separate rows unless finer map-area provenance is later required.
desc_zh: Quaternary faults offshore of California 是 USGS PCMSC 发布的加州近海第四纪断层综合数据发布，覆盖加州州海域和联邦海域的近海断层系统。数据提供线状 Shapefile、KML、FGDC 元数据和源文献 PDF，包含断层几何、属性、时代、滑动速率和参考来源，适合补足陆上加州活动断层图之外的海域断层约束。
desc_en: Quaternary faults offshore of California is a USGS PCMSC data release compiling offshore Quaternary fault systems in California State and Federal waters. It provides polyline Shapefile, KML, FGDC metadata, and source-reference PDF files with fault geometries, attributes, ages, slip-rate information, and provenance. It is the strongest open statewide offshore complement to land-based California active-fault inventories.
access_status: open public USGS ScienceBase data release; direct file package verified
source_type: official USGS ScienceBase geospatial data release
scale: statewide offshore California compilation; MultiLineString, EPSG:4326
feature_count: not counted locally; ScienceBase shapefile extension reports MultiLineString fault layer
file_size: ScienceBase aggregate zip 4,164,517 bytes; listed KML 1,607,703 bytes and shapefile member files including 245,180-byte .shp

name: Cascadia subduction zone database (2022)
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Geology, Minerals, Energy, and Geophysics Science Center / ScienceBase
theme_canonical: Cascadia subduction-zone earthquake-hazard and tectonic datasets
theme_raw: Cascadia subduction zone database: compilation of published datasets relevant to Cascadia subduction zone earthquake hazards and tectonics (2022)
coverage: Cascadia subduction zone, including Oregon, Washington, northern California and British Columbia
license: U.S. public domain / Public Domain Mark 1.0
formats: ScienceBase files, ArcGIS map package (MPK), XML metadata, ArcGIS Online web map, StoryMap, layer-level primary data links in metadata/attributes
access: USGS Science Data Catalog / ScienceBase data release; individual files downloadable; full MPK package is 3.21 GB and ScienceBase advises downloading individual files or subfolders
doi_or_citation: Staisch, L.M., and Walton, M.A. (2022), Cascadia subduction zone database: compilation of published datasets relevant to Cascadia subduction zone earthquake hazards and tectonics (2022), U.S. Geological Survey data release, https://doi.org/10.5066/P9O69X6E
time_span: Compilation publication 2022-06-22; data time period listed as 2022
notes: Broad Cascadia hazard/tectonics compilation, not a single fault-trace layer. Accepted because it aggregates onshore and offshore geologic, paleoseismic, geophysical and instrumental datasets along the Cascadia subduction zone and provides map-package/webmap access plus layer metadata pointing to primary downloads. Distinct from the CRESCENT Cascadia CFM repository already cataloged.
desc_zh: Cascadia subduction zone database (2022) 是 USGS 将 Cascadia 俯冲带相关地质、古地震、地球物理和仪器观测数据汇编到一起的数据发布，覆盖俄勒冈、华盛顿、北加州和不列颠哥伦比亚等区域。它不是单一断层线图层，而是面向俯冲带地震危险性和构造研究的开放综合数据库，提供 ScienceBase 文件、ArcGIS 地图包、在线地图和 StoryMap。
desc_en: The Cascadia subduction zone database (2022) is a USGS compilation of published geologic, paleoseismic, geophysical, and instrumental datasets along the Cascadia margin, covering Oregon, Washington, northern California, and British Columbia. It is not a single fault-trace layer; rather, it is an open integrated database for Cascadia earthquake-hazard and tectonic studies with ScienceBase files, an ArcGIS map package, web map, and StoryMap access.
access_status: open public ScienceBase data release; full package very large, individual files and metadata accessible
source_type: official USGS ScienceBase compilation data release
feature_count: not a single feature layer; multi-layer ArcGIS map package and webmap compilation
file_size: ScienceBase lists Cascadia_Database.mpk 3,444,947,251 bytes plus 7,793,274-byte webmap access docx and 129,681-byte XML metadata

name: Simplified frontal thrust splay fault geometries at the Cascadia Subduction Zone
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Earthquake Hazards Program / ScienceBase
theme_canonical: Offshore Cascadia frontal thrust splay fault geometries
theme_raw: Simplified frontal thrust splay fault geometries at the Cascadia Subduction Zone mapped using multi-channel seismic (MCS) reflection data
coverage: Cascadia Subduction Zone, U.S. Pacific Northwest offshore margin
license: U.S. public domain / Public Domain Mark 1.0
formats: CSV fault-geometry files, CSV deformation-front file, PDF references, ScienceBase aggregate zip
access: USGS Science Data Catalog and ScienceBase data release with direct CSV downloads and aggregate zip
doi_or_citation: Lucas, M., Ledeczi, A., Dunham, A.M., and Watt, J.T. (2026), Simplified frontal thrust splay fault geometries at the Cascadia Subduction Zone mapped using multi-channel seismic (MCS) reflection data, U.S. Geological Survey data release, https://doi.org/10.5066/P138BZRA
notes: Current USGS data release for six offshore frontal thrust splay faults (A-F) and a deformation-front trace. Faults A-C are mapped from CASIE21 ultra-long-offset MCS reflection data; D uses one CASIE21 line; E-F use legacy Mendocino Triple Junction data and published interpretations. The geometries were developed for 3D ground-motion simulations.
desc_zh: 该 USGS 2026 数据发布提供 Cascadia 俯冲带近海前缘逆冲分支断层的简化几何，包含 A-F 六条逆冲断层和变形前缘位置。文件以 CSV 形式给出二维解释拾取、插值后的断层几何以及参考文献，主要基于 CASIE21 多道地震反射资料和 Mendocino Triple Junction 旧资料，适合三维地震动、海啸和俯冲带断层几何建模。
desc_en: This 2026 USGS data release provides simplified geometries for six offshore frontal thrust splay faults (A-F) at the Cascadia Subduction Zone, plus the deformation-front location. CSV files include 2D seismic interpretation picks and interpolated geometries used in kinematic rupture models, drawing on CASIE21 multi-channel seismic reflection data and legacy Mendocino Triple Junction data. It is a high-value current source for Cascadia 3D ground-motion, tsunami, and subduction-zone fault-geometry modeling.
access_status: open public ScienceBase data release; direct CSV files and aggregate zip verified
source_type: official USGS ScienceBase geophysical fault-geometry data release
scale: six offshore frontal thrust fault geometries plus deformation-front trace
feature_count: 6 thrust faults (A-F) plus deformation_front.csv
file_size: ScienceBase aggregate zip 482,585 bytes; individual CSV files range from 261 bytes to 1,080,563 bytes

name: New York Bight Fault Zone interpretation shapefile
provider: U.S. Geological Survey Woods Hole Coastal and Marine Science Center
theme_canonical: Offshore fault-zone interpretation from high-resolution seismic data
theme_raw: Interpretation of the New York Bight Fault Zone on the inner-continental shelf within the New York Bight, derived from seismic data collected by the U.S. Geological Survey, 1995-1999 (Esri polyline shapefile, Geographic, WGS84)
coverage: Inner continental shelf within the New York Bight, Long Island nearshore Atlantic margin, United States
license: U.S. public domain / Public Domain Mark 1.0
formats: Esri polyline shapefile (Geographic, WGS84), USGS Open-File Report web pages and metadata
access: USGS Science Data Catalog links to USGS Open-File Report 02-152 data and metadata; GIS page/report URL verified, direct zip path not yet isolated in v013
doi_or_citation: Denny, J.F. (2002), Interpretation of the New York Bight Fault Zone on the inner-continental shelf within the New York Bight, USGS SDC record; associated with USGS Open-File Report 02-152. No registered DOI listed.
time_span: Seismic data 1995-05-01 to 1998-11-01; publication 2002; SDC metadata date 2024-03-18
notes: Accepted as a legacy but public USGS offshore fault interpretation. SDC says the New York Bight fault was clearly evident in high-resolution CHIRP, boomer and 15 cubic inch water-gun seismic records and mapped as an Esri polyline shapefile. The ScienceBase item ID form returns 404 and v013 did not locate a direct zip URL under the Open-File Report site, so future work should inspect the report media/GIS directory more deeply.
desc_zh: New York Bight Fault Zone interpretation shapefile 是 USGS Woods Hole 2002 年发布的纽约湾内陆架断层解释数据，来源于 1995-1999 年采集的 CHIRP、boomer 和小型水枪高分辨率地震资料。SDC 记录说明该断层在地震剖面中清晰可见，并被制作为 WGS84 地理坐标的 Esri 线状 Shapefile；本轮已验证 SDC 和 OFR 02-152 报告入口，但直接 zip 路径仍需后续定位。
desc_en: The New York Bight Fault Zone interpretation shapefile is a USGS Woods Hole legacy offshore-fault dataset derived from CHIRP, boomer, and small water-gun seismic data collected in 1995-1999. The SDC record states that the fault is clearly evident in the high-resolution seismic records and was mapped as an Esri polyline shapefile in geographic WGS84 coordinates. This pass verified the SDC record and OFR 02-152 report entry, while leaving direct zip-path discovery for a later follow-up.
access_status: open public USGS SDC/OFR record; direct file path needs follow-up localization
source_type: official USGS coastal and marine geoscience legacy GIS data record
scale: local offshore New York Bight inner-shelf fault-zone interpretation
feature_count: not counted in v013; SDC describes an Esri polyline shapefile
file_size: not isolated in v013; SDC describes shapefile availability through OFR 02-152

name: QUIN 1.0 Quaternary Fault Strain Indicators Database
provider: Lavecchia, Bello, Andrenacci, Cirillo, Ferrarini, Vicentini, de Nardis and Brozzetti via PANGAEA
theme_raw: QUaternary fault strain INdicators database - QUIN 1.0 - first release from the Apennines of central Italy
license: PANGAEA open data; data citation required, detailed reuse terms to follow PANGAEA license/terms
formats: PANGAEA landing page, text table, WGS84 shapefile described by metadata, Google Earth view, machine metadata JSON-LD/DataCite/ISO
access: PANGAEA DOI landing page with textfile and machine metadata downloads; dataset page describes release as .txt table and shapefile in WGS84
doi_or_citation: Lavecchia et al. (2021), QUaternary fault strain INdicators database: QUIN 1.0 - first release from the Apennines of central Italy, PANGAEA, https://doi.org/10.1594/PANGAEA.934802; related Scientific Data paper https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-022-01311-8
time_span: Published 2021-08-10; Quaternary fault-slip indicators over the last 2.5 Ma; DOI registered 2022-05-17
notes: Accepted as a fault-observation/strain-indicator dataset rather than a national fault-trace inventory. PANGAEA states QUIN 1.0 includes 3,339 fault-striation-pair records from about 455 survey sites along Quaternary hosting faults in central Italy, with geometry, kinematics, quality ranking, references and deformation-axis fields. Distinct from DISS, ITHACA, SFIT and Fault2SHA because it exposes local-scale structural observations and derived kinematic parameters.
desc_zh: QUIN 1.0 是意大利中亚平宁第四纪伸展断层带的断层擦痕/滑动指标数据库，PANGAEA 页面记录 3,339 条 Fault Striation Pair，分布在约 455 个沿断层调查站点。数据以文本表和 WGS84 Shapefile 形式发布，字段包括断层几何、运动学、质量等级、参考文献和变形轴参数；它补充的是局部构造观测和应变反演输入，而不是普通断层线清单。
desc_en: QUIN 1.0 is a Quaternary fault-slip and strain-indicator database for the central Italian intra-Apennine extensional belt. PANGAEA documents 3,339 Fault Striation Pair records from about 455 survey sites along Quaternary host faults, released as a text table and WGS84 shapefile with geometry, kinematics, quality ranking, references, and deformation-axis fields. It complements fault-source catalogues with local structural observations and kinematic inputs rather than serving as a simple fault-trace inventory.
access_status: open PANGAEA DOI record with textfile/machine metadata; shapefile described in dataset metadata
source_type: peer-reviewed PANGAEA research data repository record
scale: ~550 km along-strike central Italy intra-Apennine belt; WGS84 structural-observation shapefile/table
feature_count: 3,339 FSP records and about 455 survey sites reported by PANGAEA metadata
file_size: not summarized on PANGAEA landing page; textfile and machine metadata endpoints verified

name: Quaternary-Host Faults Database 2.0 (Southern Italy)
provider: Lavecchia, Bello, Cirillo, Pietrolungo and Brozzetti via Zenodo
theme_raw: Q-host faults database 2.0; Quaternary faults hosting QUIN 2.0 structural sites
coverage: Southern Apennines and northeastern Sicily, southern Italy
license: Zenodo open dataset; license not displayed in scraped lines, reuse terms to verify from Zenodo metadata
formats: Zenodo ZIP package, README PDF, shapefile inside ZIP per dataset description
access: Zenodo DOI landing page with downloadable ZIP and README PDF
doi_or_citation: Lavecchia, G., Bello, S., Cirillo, D., Pietrolungo, F., and Brozzetti, F. (2023), Quaternary-Host Faults Database 2.0 (Southern Italy), Zenodo, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.10370819; supplemental to QUIN 2.0, https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.8414734
time_span: Published 2023-12-13; Version v2; modified 2024-07-08
notes: Accepted as the host-fault trace layer paired with QUIN 2.0. Zenodo describes an original GIS compilation of Quaternary faults hosting or neighboring the QUIN 2.0 structural sites, following the QUIN 1.0 host-fault approach. The Zenodo page lists a 319.3 kB data ZIP and 609.0 kB README PDF. This is distinct from broader Italy source models and from QUIN 1.0 because it extends the host-fault layer into southern Italy/Sicily.
desc_zh: Quaternary-Host Faults Database 2.0 是 QUIN 2.0 的配套南意大利 host-fault 数据，记录承载或邻近 QUIN 2.0 构造站点的第四纪断层。Zenodo 页面说明该库是在 GIS 平台上编译的原始断层线数据，提供数据 ZIP 和 README PDF，适合将南亚平宁和西西里东北部的局部断层滑动观测与可制图断层迹线对应起来。
desc_en: Quaternary-Host Faults Database 2.0 is the host-fault trace companion to QUIN 2.0 for southern Italy. The Zenodo record describes an original GIS compilation of Quaternary faults hosting or neighboring QUIN 2.0 structural sites, with a data ZIP and README PDF. It is useful for linking local fault-slip observations in the southern Apennines and northeastern Sicily to mapped Quaternary fault traces.
access_status: open Zenodo landing page and download links; API returned 403 during v014 but HTML/file URLs were used for verification
source_type: Zenodo research data repository record linked to Scientific Data QUIN 2.0 article
scale: Southern Italy / Southern Apennines host-fault GIS compilation for QUIN 2.0 structural sites
feature_count: 245 linear host-fault records reported by Scientific Data QUIN 2.0 article for the related HF database
file_size: Zenodo lists 319.3 kB data ZIP and 609.0 kB README PDF; total listed files 928.3 kB

name: Colorado NGDS Active Faults MapServer
provider: Colorado Geological Survey / National Geothermal Data System / NBMG-hosted ArcGIS Server
theme_canonical: Statewide active fault traces for geothermal and hazard context
theme_raw: COActiveFaults; ActiveFault layer; Colorado active faults data collected for the National Geothermal Data System
license: Public NGDS/AASG-style ArcGIS service; explicit reuse license not stated on service metadata
formats: ArcGIS MapServer/Feature Layer, JSON query, WMS/WFS/KML capabilities described by service metadata
access: Public NBMG-hosted ArcGIS REST MapServer layer 0 with JSON query endpoint
doi_or_citation: Colorado Geological Survey active faults data for the National Geothermal Data System; CO_Data/COActiveFaults MapServer, layer ActiveFault
time_span: NGDS-era service originally published with ArcServer 10.3 metadata; current ArcGIS REST reports version 11.5
notes: Accepted as a statewide active-fault feature service distinct from the already cataloged Published Faults of the Colorado Front Range (OF-03-04), which is a regional published-fault compilation. Layer metadata states the data table includes fault location, geologic setting, position accuracy, description, displacement and citations. Count query returned 427 features. Service metadata describes WMS, WFS and KML interoperability, but v015 verified ArcGIS REST JSON only.
desc_zh: Colorado NGDS Active Faults MapServer 是 Colorado Geological Survey 为 National Geothermal Data System 提供的州级活动断层线服务，由 NBMG 托管在 ArcGIS Server 上。图层元数据说明属性表包含断层位置、地质背景、位置精度、描述、位移和引用信息，v015 的 count 查询返回 427 条要素；它补充的是全州活动断层/地热勘查背景服务，不同于已收录的 Colorado Front Range 区域已发表断层汇编。
desc_en: The Colorado NGDS Active Faults MapServer is a statewide active-fault line service supplied by the Colorado Geological Survey for the National Geothermal Data System and hosted on an NBMG ArcGIS Server. Layer metadata states that the table includes fault location, geologic setting, positional accuracy, description, displacement and citations, and the v015 count query returned 427 features. It complements the existing Colorado Front Range published-fault compilation with a statewide active-fault/geothermal-context service.
access_status: open public ArcGIS REST service; explicit reuse license not stated
source_type: official/state geological survey data exposed through NGDS ArcGIS service
feature_count: 427 features from ArcGIS returnCountOnly query
file_size: dynamic ArcGIS MapServer/Feature Layer service

name: BRGM NEOPAL_FAILLE - Recent Deformation and Paleoseismic Faults
provider: BRGM / InfoTerre Géoservices
theme_canonical: Recent deformation and paleoseismic fault traces exposed through French national geoscience OGC services
theme_raw: NEOPAL_FAILLE; Déformations récentes et paléoséismes; GéoServices risques naturels et industriels
coverage: France, with NEOPAL fault features concentrated in mapped recent-deformation and paleoseismology areas
license: BRGM public geoscience data services; reuse allowed subject to source/date attribution and metadata terms stated by InfoTerre
formats: OGC WFS 1.0/1.1 GML, OGC WMS/KML service family
access: Public BRGM InfoTerre OGC WFS layer NEOPAL_FAILLE via geoservices.brgm.fr/risques
doi_or_citation: BRGM InfoTerre Géoservices OGC risks service, NEOPAL_FAILLE layer; InfoTerre OGC service page updated 2024-03-20
time_span: recent deformation and paleoseismic evidence; service metadata current as of 2024-03-20 InfoTerre page and 2026 WFS response timestamp
notes: Accepted as a small but official French national paleoseismic/recent-deformation fault layer. InfoTerre lists NEOPAL_FAILLE under Déformations récentes et paléoséismes in the risques OGC WMS/WFS service. WFS DescribeFeatureType exposes the NEOPAL_FAILLE schema, GetFeature MAXFEATURES=1 returned a line feature with fid NEOPAL_FAILLE.PYR00010, and WFS 1.1 hits reported numberOfFeatures=8. Distinct from SEFPAF, which is a 2025 South-East France potentially active faults research dataset, and from HIKE, which is a European harmonized fault database.
desc_zh: BRGM NEOPAL_FAILLE 是法国 InfoTerre/BRGM 在“自然与工业风险”OGC 服务中发布的 recent deformation 与古地震断层线图层。InfoTerre 页面明确把 NEOPAL_FAILLE 列在“Déformations récentes et paléoséismes”下，并提供 WMS/WFS/KML 访问；v016 的 WFS schema、样例要素和 hits 查询均可访问，hits 返回 8 条要素。该层与已收录的法国东南部 SEFPAF 研究数据和欧洲 HIKE 汇编不同，适合作为法国官方古地震/近期形变线索入口。
desc_en: BRGM NEOPAL_FAILLE is a French InfoTerre/BRGM OGC layer for recent deformation and paleoseismic faults within the natural and industrial risks service. The InfoTerre service page lists NEOPAL_FAILLE under “Déformations récentes et paléoséismes” and exposes WMS/WFS/KML access; v016 checks reached the WFS schema, a sample line feature, and a WFS hits response reporting 8 features. It is distinct from the existing South-East France SEFPAF research dataset and the European HIKE compilation, serving as an official French paleoseismic/recent-deformation fault lead.
openness: open-access service with BRGM attribution/metadata reuse conditions
source_type: official national geological survey OGC service
scale: national official NEOPAL recent-deformation/paleoseismic fault layer; sparse feature set

notes: Accepted as an official Swiss national tectonic map layer exposing structural elements including named faults, normal faults, main faults and thrusts through the geo.admin.ch API metadata. The opendata.swiss description states that TK500 depicts tectonic units and structural elements of Switzerland and neighboring regions, with domains separated by major tectonic discontinuities. The API metadata for ch.swisstopo.geologie-tektonische_karte returned kind values including Bruch/Fault, Abschiebung/Normal fault, Hauptstörung/Main fault and Überschiebung/Thrust, plus named structures such as the Vuache, Lugano and Cossato-Mergozzo-Brissago faults. This is a tectonic/geological structural dataset rather than an active-fault hazard database. The opendata.swiss landing page was useful for discovery but timed out under lychee in v016, so the machine-verified primary URL is the live geo.admin.ch API endpoint.
desc_zh: Tectonic Map of Switzerland 1:500000 (TK500) 是 swisstopo/瑞士地质调查局发布的全国构造图，2024 年第 4 版覆盖瑞士及邻区的构造单元和构造线。opendata.swiss 记录说明这些构造域由主要构造不连续面分隔；v016 访问的 geo.admin.ch API 元数据返回了 Fault、Normal fault、Main fault、Thrust 等类别，以及 Vuache Fault、Lugano Fault、Cossato-Mergozzo-Brissago Fault 等命名构造。该数据适合补充瑞士国家级构造断裂/逆冲线索，但不是专门的活动断层危险性数据库。
desc_en: The Tectonic Map of Switzerland 1:500000 (TK500) is an official swisstopo/Swiss Geological Survey national tectonic map, with the 4th edition updated in 2024 for Switzerland and neighboring regions. The opendata.swiss record describes tectonic domains separated by major tectonic discontinuities, and the v016 geo.admin.ch API metadata returned structural categories such as fault, normal fault, main fault and thrust, plus named structures including the Vuache, Lugano and Cossato-Mergozzo-Brissago faults. It is useful as a national tectonic structural-line dataset rather than as a dedicated active-fault hazard database.

The current GTK ArcGIS REST service exposes Bedrock of Finland scale free as a group layer with Fault structures (layer 28) and Finstruct fault structures (layer 29). Layer metadata identifies both as polyline feature layers and lists fault categories including unspecified major fault zone, major dextral and sinistral strike-slip fault zones, thrust fault zone, major normal fault zone, major detachment fault, and major oblique-slip fault zone. Count queries returned 12,086 features for layer 28 and 4,229 features for layer 29. This is a national structural-geology/bedrock fault service rather than a dedicated active-fault hazard database.

desc_en: GTK Bedrock of Finland Fault Structures and Finstruct Fault Structures are national bedrock structural fault layers exposed by the Geological Survey of Finland through the MDaE GeologicalMaps ArcGIS REST service. v017 verified layer 28, Fault structures, and layer 29, Finstruct fault structures, both as polyline Feature Layers with categories such as major strike-slip, thrust, normal, detachment and oblique-slip fault zones; count queries returned 12,086 and 4,229 features respectively. The dataset is useful for Finnish structural geology and bedrock fault analysis rather than as a dedicated active-fault hazard inventory.

desc_en: GeoSphere Austria Geology 1:1,000,000 Tectonic Lines is an official Austrian national geology/tectonic map service from GeoSphere Austria and the Geological Survey of Austria. v017 verified layer 0, Tektonische Linien, as a polyline Feature Layer whose classes include confirmed faults, inferred faults and first- or second-order nappe boundaries; a count query returned 673 features and sample attributes include fault number, classification, name, fault-system hierarchy, shear zone, region and reference. The row records the national Austrian source service separately from the already cataloged European HIKE compilation.

The product is a general bedrock/structural geology dataset rather than a dedicated active-fault inventory.

notes: Accepted as the detailed companion to NGU Bedrock N250. NGU describes Bedrock N50 as consisting of polygons/surfaces and boundaries based on the printed 1:50,000 bedrock geological map series and suitable for municipal planning, natural resources, construction, alternative energy and assessment of natural hazards of earthquakes and landslides. The same NGU download page states NLOD licensing and offers SOSI, Shapefile and File Geodatabase formats. The Atom feed for UUID a5c76d05-33bd-4a1d-b28b-81575092e468 contains 128 entries including a nationwide geographic-coordinate FileGDB fileproxy. This is a detailed geological boundaries/structures product rather than a curated active-fault database. The nationwide FileGDB fileproxy returned HTTP 200 in a direct HEAD probe but timed out under lychee, so it is documented in notes rather than retained in the verified v017 URL list. The specific Atom feed UUID a5c76d05-33bd-4a1d-b28b-81575092e468 was parsed successfully during discovery and contained 128 entries, but the feed timed out in final lychee, so the row primary URL is the stable dataset-specific NGU download selection page.

desc_en: NGU Bedrock Geology N50 is the Geological Survey of Norway’s detailed 1:50,000 bedrock geology dataset, consisting of polygons/surfaces and boundaries based on the printed bedrock geological map series. It is a detailed geological boundaries/structures product rather than a curated active-fault hazard catalogue.

notes: Accepted as a statewide Minnesota Geological Survey fault layer. ArcGIS REST layer metadata states that it depicts faults in Paleozoic bedrock units, largely inferred from drill-hole data and only very rarely exposed in outcrop; most faults are vertical and represent dip-slip offset generally less than 100 feet. It is a polyline Feature Layer, and a count query returned 903 features. A newer D-05 Minnesota Bedrock Geology 2025 ArcGIS item was found, but it is a Map Package without a direct feature-service URL in the item JSON, so this v018 row uses the directly queryable S-21 fault layer. The legacy purl.umn.edu/101466 citation in the layer metadata redirected to a 403 response under lychee and is kept only as citation text, not as a verified v018 URL.
desc_zh: Minnesota Bedrock Geology Paleozoic Faults 是 Minnesota Geological Survey 的 S-21 明尼苏达基岩地质图中的古生代基岩断层线图层。ArcGIS REST 元数据说明这些断层主要根据钻孔资料推断，露头很少，多数为近垂直、位移通常小于 100 英尺的倾滑断层；v018 的 count 查询返回 903 条 polyline 要素。它是州级基岩结构/古生代断层数据，不是活动断层危险性数据库。
desc_en: Minnesota Bedrock Geology Paleozoic Faults is the Minnesota Geological Survey S-21 bedrock geology fault layer for Paleozoic bedrock units. The ArcGIS REST metadata states that the faults are largely inferred from drill-hole data, are rarely exposed in outcrop, and are mostly vertical dip-slip faults with generally less than 100 feet of offset; the v018 count query returned 903 polyline features. It is a statewide bedrock-structure/Paleozoic fault dataset rather than an active-fault hazard inventory.

AZGS publications page links the Natural Hazards in Arizona viewer and states it showcases earth fissures, active faults and earthquakes. The Experience Builder data references a Web Map with an Active Faults layer, and ArcGIS item 2dfd9712f644452597fd20e02c49c5c4 describes active faults throughout Arizona, derived from AZGS and modified from Pearthree 1998. The layer is an EPSG:4326 polyline Feature Layer named activefaults; a count query returned 1,781 features, and sample fields include fullname, featureuri, feature type, geologic history, age fields, observation, position accuracy, displacement and sliprate. This row supersedes the earlier dead/unresponsive AASG AZActiveFaults service as the verified Arizona active-fault route. The human-facing Experience Builder URL timed out under lychee and is retained only as discovery context via the ArcGIS item/sharing REST evidence.

desc_en: The Arizona Natural Hazards Viewer Active Faults Feature Service is the current public AZGS/University of Arizona active-fault layer for Arizona. time_span: Geological map resurveyed in 2021-2022 and published in 2022 according to the Continental Shelf Department; EGDI metadata publication 2024-04-05, revision 2026-03-17
notes: Accepted through the EGDI metadata and haleconnect service route because the Malta Data Portal and Continental Shelf Department download pages are Cloudflare/form gated from the local verification environment. Search/browser evidence shows that the Malta Data Portal describes the dataset as polyline fault features available as ESRI shapefiles, and the Continental Shelf Department page states that the 1:10,000 geological map has seven GIS layers including fault features, with download links provided after a form submission. For the verified catalog row, v023 used only links that lychee could validate: EGDI metadata plus WFS/WMS GetCapabilities. The WFS capabilities title is GE.Faults_10k, provider is Planning Authority Malta, fees are noConditionsApply, and a WFS 2.0 hits query for ge:MappedFeature returned 711 features. The WFS feature-type bounding box in the capabilities response contains a suspicious lower latitude value, so coverage is described from the metadata/portal rather than the raw capabilities bbox.
desc_zh: Geological Map of the Maltese Islands - Faults WFS 是马耳他地质图中的断层要素服务，覆盖 Malta、Gozo 和 Comino。EGDI metadata 将其描述为 1:10,000 比例尺的 polyline 断层数据，表示地质单元之间的构造接触或断裂位移面，并标注 no conditions apply、no limitations to public access。v023 验证了 haleconnect WFS/WMS GetCapabilities，WFS 标题为 `GE.Faults_10k`，对 `ge:MappedFeature` 的 hits 查询返回 711 条要素；官方下载页说明 shapefile 可通过表单获取，但该下载路径未能通过本机 lychee 复核。
desc_en: The Geological Map of the Maltese Islands - Faults WFS exposes the fault layer of the Maltese geological map for Malta, Gozo and Comino. EGDI metadata describes it as a 1:10,000 polyline dataset representing structural contacts and planes of movement/failure between geological units, with no conditions applying and no limitations to public access. v023 verified the haleconnect WFS/WMS GetCapabilities endpoints; the WFS title is `GE.Faults_10k`, and a WFS hits query for `ge:MappedFeature` returned 711 features. The official download pages document ESRI shapefile availability through a form-provided link, but that download route was not lychee-verified locally.
access_status: open public INSPIRE/OGC WFS/WMS service; shapefile download documented but form-gated/not lychee-verified
openness: open-access service with noConditionsApply/no public-access limitations in EGDI metadata
source_type: official INSPIRE/EGDI metadata record and OGC WFS/WMS service

name: Oklahoma Geological Survey Fault Database and Fault Catalogs
provider: Oklahoma Geological Survey, University of Oklahoma; Oklahoma Corporation Commission / ArcGIS Hub service mirror
theme_canonical: Oklahoma statewide fault database, interpretive fault map, preliminary fault map, industry-contributed faults and optimal fault orientations
theme_raw: Oklahoma Fault Database; OF2-2016 Comprehensive Fault Database and Interpretive Fault Map; OF1-2016 industry contributions; OF3-2015 Preliminary Fault Map; OF4-2015 optimal fault orientations; OGS Fault Layers FeatureServer
license: Public OGS web downloads and ArcGIS hosted feature service; explicit reuse license not found in v024, so detailed license terms should be verified with OGS/OCC/OU
formats: Official GIS-compatible ZIP downloads; PDF plates/reports; ArcGIS REST Feature Service; supported hosted exports include CSV, shapefile, SQLite, GeoPackage, file geodatabase, feature collection, GeoJSON, KML and Excel
access: Official Oklahoma Geological Survey Fault Database web page with GIS ZIP downloads, plus public OGS Fault Layers ArcGIS FeatureServer with query/export support
doi_or_citation: Marsh, S. and Holland, A.A., 2016, Comprehensive Fault Database and Interpretive Fault Map of Oklahoma, Oklahoma Geological Survey Open-File Report OF2-2016; Holloway, Holland and Keller, 2016, OF1-2016; Darold and Holland, 2015, OF4-2015; Holland, 2015, OF3-2015
time_span: OGS fault database page updated 2022-04-12; main cited open-file reports published 2015-2016; ArcGIS hosted service item created 2020 and modified 2021 according to sharing REST metadata
notes: Accepted as the official Oklahoma Geological Survey fault database/download hub with a public service mirror. The OGS page states that an interpreted fault map was compiled from oil and gas industry data and published literature and published as OF2-2016, that most faults from published literature are documented in OF2-2016 and the OF3-2015 supplement, and that the map is compiled from the Oklahoma Fault Database, which continues to be updated as additional information is available. The official page provides GIS-compatible ZIP downloads for OF2-2016, OF1-2016, OF4-2015 and OF3-2015. The ArcGIS Hub item `OGS Fault Layers` says the layers are representative of data derived from the Oklahoma Geological Survey fault catalogs, and its FeatureServer exposes OF2-2016, OF2-2016_Interpretative and OF4-2015 polyline layers. v024 count queries returned 19,054 features for OF2-2016, 5,877 for OF2-2016_Interpretative and 26,313 for OF4-2015. A separate Oklahoma DEQ geologic faults MapServer layer, copyright Oklahoma Geological Survey, returned 4,538 features and was retained as supporting evidence rather than a separate row to avoid fragmenting the Oklahoma canonical source.
desc_zh: Oklahoma Geological Survey Fault Database and Fault Catalogs 是俄克拉荷马州级断层数据库与开放文件报告 GIS 数据入口。OGS 页面说明，OF2-2016 解释性断层图由油气行业资料和公开文献汇编，许多文献来源断层记录在 OF2-2016 与 OF3-2015 补充材料中，并且 Oklahoma Fault Database 会随着新增断层信息持续更新；页面直接提供 OF2-2016、OF1-2016、OF4-2015 和 OF3-2015 的 GIS-compatible ZIP 下载。v024 同时验证了 ArcGIS Hub 的 OGS Fault Layers FeatureServer：OF2-2016、OF2-2016_Interpretative 和 OF4-2015 均为 polyline 图层，count 查询分别返回 19,054、5,877 和 26,313 条要素。
desc_en: The Oklahoma Geological Survey Fault Database and Fault Catalogs page is the state-level OGS entry point for Oklahoma fault GIS data and related open-file reports. The OGS page states that the OF2-2016 interpretive fault map was compiled from oil-and-gas industry data and published literature, that many literature-derived faults are documented in OF2-2016 and the OF3-2015 supplement, and that the Oklahoma Fault Database continues to be updated as new fault information becomes available; it provides GIS-compatible ZIP downloads for OF2-2016, OF1-2016, OF4-2015 and OF3-2015. v024 also verified the ArcGIS Hub OGS Fault Layers FeatureServer, where OF2-2016, OF2-2016_Interpretative and OF4-2015 are polyline layers with count-query totals of 19,054, 5,877 and 26,313 features respectively.
access_status: open public OGS download page and public ArcGIS FeatureServer; license-to-verify
openness: open-access downloads and services, explicit reuse license not found in v024

feature_count: ArcGIS FeatureServer counts in v024: OF2-2016 19,054; OF2-2016_Interpretative 5,877; OF4-2015 26,313. Supporting Oklahoma DEQ/OGS geologic faults MapServer count: 4,538.
